issue_title: Cloud Directory should accept None as NextToken
issue_body:
Let's say your listing and index or something, and want to iterate the pages:

```python
next = None
while True:
    resp = client.list_index(
        DirectoryArn='...',
        RangesOnIndexedValues=[...]
        IndexReference={'Selector': '..'},
        NextToken=next
    )
    next = resp.get('NextToken')
    if not next:
        break
```

The above doesn't work as boto doesn't accept `None` as NextToken value (it nags about it not being string, iirc). So I would have to:

```python
next = None
while True:
    if next:
        resp = client.list_index(
            DirectoryArn='...',
            RangesOnIndexedValues=[...]
            IndexReference={'Selector': '..'},
            NextToken=next
        )
    else:
        resp = client.list_index(
            DirectoryArn='...',
            RangesOnIndexedValues=[...]
            IndexReference={'Selector': '..'}
            # omit it here
        )

    next = resp.get('NextToken')
    if not next:
        break
```

Which is pretty annoying. Workaround is to:

```python
next = None
while True:
    params = dict(
        DirectoryArn='...',
        RangesOnIndexedValues=[...]
        IndexReference={'Selector': '..'}
    )
    if next_token:
        params['NextToken'] = next
    resp = client.list_index(**params)

    next = resp.get('NextToken')
    if not next:
        break
```

Which is pretty ugly, plus you lose auto-completion (which actually doesn't exist due to https://github.com/boto/boto3/issues/1055).

issue_title: asyncio task parenting not serializing parent service name
issue_body:
so currently if you patch asyncio it will enable linking of contexts via _wrapped_create_task.  The way it works is that it will create a new Context with the trace and span IDs from the current_task.  The issue with this is that when you start a span, it will get the trace_id and span_id of the context, however the service name is lost, so the parent's span's service name will not get propagated if the context was created via _wrapped_create_task.

This is really important because if the service is not set then the span will get silently dropped.

I suggest fixing this by storing the parent's service name in the context.  I'll create a PR shortly to solve this.

self.socket.setsockopt(socket.IPPROTO_IPV6, socket.IPV6_V6ONLY, 1)

issue_title: Use Siphon to obtain useful WRF initialization grids from TDS
issue_body:
The WRF initialization process relies heavily on GRIB data...this is a travesty, and is an issue that the WRF group does not think is an issue (because people only want to initialize WRF using grids that are FTP'd from NCEP, right?).

Thankfully, the data from the GRIB file is quickly extracted and put into a temporary file early in the WRF initialization process using the `UNGRIB` program.This feature would do the following in an attempt to circumvent the `UNGRIB` step of WRF initialization. This would allow users to utilize the netCDF Subset Service of the TDS to request and transfer only the data that is needed to spin-up WRF.
1. Use WRF v-tables to make NCSS request for only the grids that WRF needs for initialization (no need to download every grid available from the model run).
2. Allow the user to subset temporally and spatially to further cut down on data transferred over the network (possibly reading directly from the WRF config file).
3. Return a compressed netCDF-4 file containing those grids (even more reduction in data transferred over the network)
4. Extract the netCDF-4 data into a binary file following the expected output by `UNGRIB`, which is written as fortran unformatted writes.

A side benefit of step 4. is that users could potentially take their own netCDF files and get them into the format needed by the `METGRID` step of the WRF initialization process, thus making the process of using your their own, non-GRIB data to initialize WRF easier (for example, several climate models only output netCDF, not GRIB).

issue_title: "Loading .env environment variables" logs to stderr
issue_body:
This is a minor annoyance, as in some scripts we log stderr. 
I think this should be changed, as it is not an error message.

Thanks in advance!

https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/4b95004f1aca99006a72789804a0d19cac95386c/pipenv/core.py#L166

issue_title: Allow per-project pipenv configuration in Pipfile, ".pipenv.cfg", setup.cfg, or some other per-project configuration file
issue_body:
There are quite a few `pipenv` configuration options presented as environment variables. I would like to be able to set these on a per-project basis, committed to version control, loaded and understood by `pipenv` itself and not by a secondary tool.

The [documentation section for them](https://pipenv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/advanced/#configuration-with-environment-variables) recommends using direnv. While I'm sure direnv is a lovely solution, it's another dependency for developer machines and for any infrastructure where we want to use `pipenv`.
I don't think it's unreasonable to want `pipenv` to be able to have its own config file or read from an existing config like `setup.cfg` or even a special section of `Pipfile`.

I'd suggest even using a section of `pyproject.toml`, but that seems downright confusing to mix with `Pipfile`.
One easy way out is to just load a special `.pipenv.cfg` with configparser.

Allowing the use of `--config FILENAME` to use a custom config file location would be a logical added goody.

More than anything else, I want to be able to set `PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT=1` in my projects, especially since `/.venv/` is in my `.gitignore`.

Apologies if this has been requested before, but I don't see it in any of the closed issues.
I'm happy to work on this in a PR if it gets the ol' 👍 from people.

issue_title: Install local whl doesn't install that wheel's dependices
issue_body:
When installing a .whl package that we built using `pipenv install package.whl` it doesn't install the dependencies in package.whl.

These are the required packages in package.whl setup.py file:
`install_requires=[
        'requests==2.18.4',
        'PySocks==1.6.7',
        'nose==1.3.7',
        'redis==2.10.6',
        'Sphinx==1.6.4',
        'twine==1.9.1',
    ]
`

Step to replicate:
`pipenv install /path/to/package.whl`  # Doesn't install dependencies
`pip install /path/to/package.whl`        # Does install package.whl dependencies

issue_title: support for multiple sources
issue_body:
I have tried to define two sources in Pipfile, something like this:

```toml
[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"

[[source]]
url = "https://myprivatepypi.com/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "internal"

[requires]
python_version = "2.7"

[dev-packages]
"coverage" = {version="*", index="pypi"}

[packages]
"my-private-package" = {version="*", index="internal"}
```

However, when I try `pipenv install` I run into an error, which includes a 404, saying that pipenv is looking for 'my-private-package' in https://pypi.python.org/simple.

https://github.com/kennethreitz/pipenv/blob/1faec7f251780e318264dee0699e6936e0c2000b/pipenv/project.py#L412-L413

Seems like the code might just be grabbing the first source in the Pipfile?

issue_title: running the tests creates a .venv in the repo dir
Pipenv install fails with traceback on Ubuntu 16.04.4
issue_body:
I am converting an already-running django project on an Ubuntu server over to `pipenv`. I converted my `requirements.txt` file over to `Pipfile` and `Pipfile.lock` on my local system, and it's running fine. When I ssh into the server, pull down my code, and run `pipenv install`, the system can hang for over 30 minutes (usually I stop the VM after a few minutes).

Please run `$ python -m pipenv.help`, and paste the results here.

<details><summary>$ python -m pipenv.help output</summary>

Pipenv version: `'11.10.0'`

Pipenv location: `'/home/innovationuser/.pyenv/versions/3.6.4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv'`

Other Python installations in `PATH`:

  - `2.7`: `/usr/bin/python2.7`
  - `2.7`: `/usr/bin/python2.7`
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/innovationuser/.pyenv/versions/3.6.4/lib/python3.6/runpy.py", line 193, in _run_module_as_main
    "__main__", mod_spec)
  File "/home/innovationuser/.pyenv/versions/3.6.4/lib/python3.6/runpy.py", line 85, in _run_code
    exec(code, run_globals)
  File "/home/innovationuser/.pyenv/versions/3.6.4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/help.py", line 89, in <module>
    main()
  File "/home/innovationuser/.pyenv/versions/3.6.4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/help.py", line 24, in main
    found = find_python_in_path(python_v)
  File "/home/innovationuser/.pyenv/versions/3.6.4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/core.py", line 355, in find_python_in_path
    version = python_version(p)
  File "/home/innovationuser/.pyenv/versions/3.6.4/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/utils.py", line 218, in python_version
    return u'{major}.{minor}.{micro}'.format(**version)
TypeError: format() argument after ** must be a mapping, not NoneType

</details>

------------

##### Expected result

Create a new .venv with dependencies.

##### Actual result

Hangs the server for 30+ minutes with what seems to be pegged disk read bytes (nearly 200MB/min) (minimal network activity and <10% CPU usage).

issue_title: Risk of outdated Pipfile.lock after editing Pipfile.
issue_body:
In addition #8, this is perhaps the most serious usability problem with the current approach: For a developer it is very easy to forget about `Pipfile.lock` after editing `Pipfile`, especially because he is not supposed to care about the lock file.

This is not an issue with pure approaches (`setup.py` or `requirements.txt`) because there is only one version of truth. Pipfile introduces an intermediate step that translates one truth into another, and different tools look at different files. This just asks for trouble. I can already see the broken builds and "fix: Forgot to build Pipfile" commits waiting to happen.

A very common workflow is to install a dependency manually, check if it works, and then add it to `requirements.txt` or just call `pip freeze` again. With Pidfiles, there is no `pip freeze` (see #8) and you have to invoke a command-line utility after editing the `Pidfile` to make things happen. Unfortunately, you just manually installed the dependency, so everything works anyway (on your computer) and there is no indication that your `Pidfile.lock` is out of sync if you forget this step. You are a single `git commit -a` away from a broken build.

Build or install tools (`pip`) cannot check if `Pipfile` and `Pipfile.lock` are out of sync without executing the `Pipfile` (which is bad, see #7) and cannot warn the user.

issue_title: url vs. base_url in extruct.extract
issue_body: Right now ``extruct.extract`` as an ``url`` parameter which is documented as "url of the html documents", but in reality it's used as a base url (at least in ``LxmlMicrodataExtractor``, maybe in others as well). I think we should check if it's indeed always used as a base url, update documentation and introduce ``base_url`` argument deprecating ``url``? Another option would be to extract ``base_url`` in extruct, but this feels like worse solution to me (what if caller already has base_url or has more accurate base_url?), although we could also support both base_url and url.

issue_title: js-yaml 4 tails
issue_body:
Summary of existng design issues. Those should be solved to make a new major version.

### General

- [ ] Safe by default, see #330 
  - Revisit current schemas & JS specific things.
  - Should we drop JS/Regexp to external packages as custom types?
  - load/dump - new method names?
- [ ] Consider change parser approach
  - seems we have unresolveable problems like #62 , ? #292 
  - we could fallback to reference model with events layer
  - we could store intermadiate tokens in typed array and get much more effecient scanner (see CSSTree approach)
- [ ] Better error messages for extra EOL, #218 (ask spec devs)
- [ ] # (ask spec devs)
- [ ] (ask spec devs)
- [ ] (ask spec devs)
- [ ] ? #257
- [ ] ? #253
- [ ] ? #301, #319
- [ ] ? #307 

### Javascrpt-related

- [ ] It would be nice to have `.strinigify()` / `.parse()` methods, like JSON.
  - exact JSON schema?
  - what about Date / Regexp support?
- [ ] Map / Set support
  - How to distinguish map syntax? Users prefer Object in most cases - much more convenient.
  - What about old browsers? We could suggest use Map / Set polyfills, but some basic (SJON-like) functionality should work out of box.
  - also see #169
- [ ] Binary data - where to map? Is `Uint8Array` ok or we should use Buffer in node.js?
- #164

### Unsure

- #280

Status: there's a working `RdkSimpleConsumer` which derives from `pykafka.SimpleConsumer` without changing the interface (see [here][0]). My `%timeit` results are somewhat noisy, but I generally see 4-7 microsecs per `consume` call vs 25-50 microsecs for the pure-python consumer (where me == @yungchin, as I've just edited this summary...).

[0]: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/e475d9703f7903e1e9272503583f2ec45f8f21ce/pykafka/rdkafka/simple_consumer.py#L5

Still to do:
- [ ] passing `SimpleConsumer.__init__` config settings into the underlying rdkafka consumer
- [ ] wrap error conditions in the C module in meaningful exceptions (I've marked these `TODO` in the code)
- [ ] figure out if the python side needs more exceptions out of the rdkafka consumer: most cluster reconfiguration error responses and the like are absorbed as a matter of course, so I'm wondering if `KafkaClient.cluster` and co will need more explicit updates to stay informed?
- [ ] integration testing (there's a separate issue for this)
- [ ] hook up logging (also has a separate issue)
- [ ] take a shot at redefining `_rd_kafka.Message` to expose the payload through the buffer API rather than doing a string copy

issue_title: threshold_triangle fails for non-integer images
issue_body:
## Description
Thresholding with the triangle method can fail or give unreliable results for non-integer images. This is because of how the histogram is constructed, and because downstream of that there are a few places where bin number is equated with image data values. Observed when trying to use skimage.filters.try_all_threshold(). 

I'd be happy to make a PR with a fix.

## Way to reproduce
```python
from skimage.filters import thresholding

image = skimage.data.text()

# expected behavior, from the unit test
thresholding.threshold_triangle(image)
104

# by default uses 256 bins
thresholding.threshold_triangle(image.astype(float))
191.365234375

# should bin the same as when it's an integer image
thresholding.threshold_triangle(image.astype(float), nbins=image.max())
147.474619289

# expected behavior, from the unit test
thresholding.threshold_triangle(np.invert(image))
151

# bin centers are not integers but are used as indices
thresholding.threshold_triangle(np.invert(image).astype(float))
IndexError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-110-04d94652cd41> in <module>()
----> 1 thresholding.threshold_triangle(np.invert(image).astype(float))

/python2.7/site-packages/skimage/filters/thresholding.pyc in threshold_triangle(image, nbins)
    720     width = arg_peak_height - arg_low_level
    721     x1 = np.arange(width)
--> 722     y1 = hist[x1 + arg_low_level]
    723 
    724     # Normalize.

IndexError: arrays used as indices must be of integer (or boolean) type
```

issue_title: Lack of symmetry in find_boundaries?
issue_body:
I'm trying to use the find_boundaries function, and to figure out what output to expect, I tested it on an array with only a single pixel being ``True`` and the rest being ``False``:

```python
import numpy as np
from skimage.segmentation import find_boundaries

a = np.zeros((5, 5), dtype='bool')
a[2, 2] = True

boundary = find_boundaries(a)
print(boundary)
```
The output was the following:
```python
[[False False False False False]
 [False False False False False]
 [False False  True  True False]
 [False False  True False False]
 [False False False False False]]
```

I was surprised by this output, as it seems to me that it is less symmetric than it ought to be. I was expecting either just a single pixel being ``True``, or perhaps a 2x2 block being ``True``. I decided to test the symmetry a bit further by finding the boundary of the same array after flipping it:

```python
boundary_inverted = find_boundaries(a[::-1, ::-1])[::-1, ::-1]
print(boundary_inverted)
print(np.all(boundary == boundary_inverted))
```
The output was
```python
[[False False False False False]
 [False False  True False False]
 [False  True  True False False]
 [False False False False False]
 [False False False False False]]
False
```

I'm not sure if this is a bug, but it may well be. The docs could certainly be improved, though, as I cannot understand why the result is what it is.

Can anyone please help me clarify what to expect here?

issue_title: Add uint16 images' support for co-occurrence matrix
issue_body:
## Description
The co-occurrence matrix implemented in feature/texture.py (skimage.feature.greycomatrix) does not accept images with more than 256 levels. This is reasonable for the calculation of Haralick features, but I think that it might be useful to remove this limitation for other use cases. 

As an example, I am currently working on an application, where I want to build a neighboring graph of several objects in an image. For this, I calculate a Voronoï tessellation (objects are the seeds), which partitions the image plane into labeled regions. Finding touching regions corresponds to identifying pairs of values with co-occurrence > 0. The following code does that: 

```
cooc = skimage.feature.greycomatrix(
    labels, [1],
    [0, np.pi/4, np.pi/2, 3*np.pi/4, np.pi, 5*np.pi/4, 3*np.pi/2, 7*np.pi/4],
    levels=labels.max() + 1
)

cooc = np.sum(cooc[:,:,0,:], axis=2)
cooc = cooc - np.diag(np.diag(cooc))
adjmat = cooc>0
```

Here `labels` is a label image (here an output of a Voronoï tessellation). Obviously, this only works if there are less than 256 objects in the image, due to the limitation of skimage.feature.greycomatrix.

issue_title: Make instantiation with credentials simpler/cleaner
issue_body:
We've just started to use Hawk for the Treeherder API and so are having to add support to our Python client that we make available for people to interact with the API, as well as update documentation for it.

The current WIP docs for how people should use our Python client, has examples like:
```python
from requests_hawk import HawkAuth
from thclient import TreeherderClient
...
auth = HawkAuth(credentials={
    'id': 'your-treeherder-client-id',
    'key': 'your-secret',
    'algorithm': 'sha256'
})
client = TreeherderClient(protocol='https', host='treeherder.mozilla.org', auth=auth)
client.post_collection('mozilla-central', tjc)
```
This is more verbose than I'd like (users of our client shouldn't have to know we're using sha256) and passing the credentials as a dict doesn't seem very clean, so I was contemplating wrapping HawkAuth like so in our client code:
```python
class TreeherderHawkAuth(HawkAuth):
    """Thin wrapper around HawkAuth to reduce boilerplate."""
    def __init__(self, client_id, secret):
        credentials = {
            'id': client_id,
            'key': secret,
            'algorithm': 'sha256'
        }
        return super(TreeherderHawkAuth, self).__init__(credentials=credentials)
```
So instead the example would become:
```python
from thclient import TreeherderClient, TreeherderHawkAuth
...
auth = TreeherderHawkAuth('your-treeherder-client-id', 'your-secret')
client = TreeherderClient(protocol='https', host='treeherder.mozilla.org', auth=auth)
client.post_collection('mozilla-central', tjc)
```

However we then have a wrapper around a wrapper whose purpose was to make using Hawk with requests simpler, which seems to defeat the point a bit.

Would you be open to making changes to make this unnecessary? (eg defaulting to 'sha256' if no algorithm specified, being able to pass the credentials in a nicer form than a dict etc)

Thanks :-)

issue_title: travis: remove pypy from test matrix
after_record_update and previous version
issue_body:
In the receivers of `after_record_update` signal, in INSPIRE, we need to know what has changed in the record in order to propagate certain modifications (e.g. say a record initially refers to A, B, C records, and subsequently it is modified to refer instead to records B, C, and D: we need to programmatically reindex records A and D in order to recompute the number of citations).

For this reason in the unsupported Invenio 2.x we are patching invenio-records to take a snapshot of the original record before modification and send it as a second parameter when calling `after_record_update`.

In Invenio 3.x how this requirement will be supported? Is there a way for a receiver to retrieve easily the previous version of the record before the current update? I see there is a `revisions` property that can be queried, however this seems a bit heavier for the purpose (as one would need to iterate all the revisions in order to obtain the last-before-the-current-one).

cc: @tomaszgy

issue_title: tests: simplify doctest execution
issue_labels: [{'id': 414883496, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-accounts/labels/Size:%20easy', 'name': 'Size: easy', 'color': 'bc3cbc', 'default': False}, {'id': 414883430, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-accounts/labels/Status:%20ready%20for%20work', 'name': 'Status: ready for work', 'color': '149414', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: pkg_resources causes a slow import; any way to avoid?
issue_body:
The use of pkg_resources.parse_version (see #162) causes the import time of numexpr to slow down significantly, from approx 0.4 seconds without pkg_resources.parse_version to 2.1 seconds with pkg_resources.parse_version.

(perhaps I should note that I am not a direct consumer of numexpr and have never used it, but pandas does and I use pandas)

Please consider alternate methods... if there are any.

The culprit is in expressions.py

```
import numpy
from pkg_resources import parse_version
_np_version = parse_version(numpy.__version__)
```

(my "speedup" was to comment out those last two lines referencing parse_version and substitute `_np_version = '1.12.1'` just to see how this impacts import time)

----

(see also https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/510)

issue_title: Numexpr fails to install from source
issue_body:
From a fresh install (or virtualenv), numexpr fails to install from source. The following error occurs:
```shell
$ python setup.py install
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "setup.py", line 223, in <module>
    setup_package()
  File "setup.py", line 62, in setup_package
    from numpy.distutils.core import setup
ImportError: No module named numpy.distutils.core
```

Specifically the Line 62 in setup.py (https://github.com/pydata/numexpr/blob/master/setup.py#L62) presumes numpy is already installed before even running `setup` itself. I doubt this was intended since numpy is also listed inside `setup_requires`.

This issue seems to be related to https://github.com/PyTables/PyTables/issues/454

issue_title: Icon Request: File Extension Aliases
issue_body:
This is not a request for a new icon so much as it is a request for new aliases to existing icons. It would be great if file type icons could be referenced by their corresponding file extensions. 

Here's why.

Let's say I have a library of files in my CMS and I want to display the appropriate file icon next to each file shown on a page. Currently I have to write a bunch of `if...then` conditions in my template to plug the right keyword ('excel', 'word', 'pdf') into the `fa-file-xxx-o` class.

If file type icons could be aliased by file extension, then I could simply output `fa-file-{ myFile.extension }-o` as the class and much magic would happen! When a Word doc is displayed, the above template code would output `fa-file-doc-o` or `fa-file-docx-o`, both of which would be aliases for `fa-file-word-o`. It would really help make my template code more efficient.

I would take as many as I could get, but of particular interest to me are doc, docx, xls, xlsx, ppt, pptx, and mp4. I also use .zip and .pdf extensively, but happily, these already work that way!

Thanks so much for keeping Font Awesome awesome!

issue_title: Running patronictl list on a leader on a Consul cluster removes the leader's lock
issue_body:
We are using Consul as DCS and noticed that running `patronictl list -c ...yml clustername` on a cluster leader using its .yml will demote the leader dirtily, giving no warning and causing a failover.

The cause seems to be this:
https://github.com/zalando/patroni/blob/master/patroni/dcs/consul.py#L157

Patronictl will create a new session with the name specified on the .yml (the leader one), look for existing leader sessions, find one with matching name but different session ID (the one from the actual Patroni daemon) and decide to remove the lock.

It seems this behavior might make sense if it runs on the daemon itself, but definitely not on patronictl

issue_title: better support for distribution of pyrobuf processed content in packages
issue_body:
I'm trying to work out how I can distribute a package that includes a proto module that needs to be built with ``pyrobuf``. In my distributed package I want to be able to access the generated module from within my package namespace using something like ``from mypackage.subpackage import generated_proto_module``. I can't work out how to do this using ``pyrobuf``.

This use case seem reasonable to me, but perhaps I'm missing something in ``pyrobuf`` that would let me achieve this. I can't find any examples showing how I could achieve this using the ``pyrobuf`` tooling. 

Reading the docs it appears that ``pyrobuf`` CLI will install each ``.proto`` file as a completely separate Python package, but I want any generated modules to be located within my package namespace.

I think I need to use ``pyrobuf`` to process my ``.proto`` into ``.pyx`` and ``.pxd`` files, which I would then add to the project source tree as version controlled files which would be distributed. The extension would get built when the package is being installed on a client machine. Assuming this approach makes sense...

The problem I'm having is trying to determine how to use ``pyrobuf`` to process the ``.proto`` file in such a way that I can include the ``.pyx`` and ``.pxd`` files in my distribution so they can be built into an extension when the package is installed on the client machine. At present the CLI ``__main__`` seems hard coded to always goes one step too far (at least for my use case) by always building the extension. I think what I need is the output of the ``gen_message`` function but excluding the ``cythonize`` and ``setup``. 

Perhaps the CLI ``__main__`` could be modified to build the extension based on an extra flag (e.g. ``--build``) similar to the ``install`` flag? That way it would work as before but also for my use case. I could say ``--build=False`` so I just end up with the ``.pyx`` and ``.pxd`` files.

issue_title: endif(expression) a notice?
issue_body:
Heya,

I just hit the pretty obscure notice "notice: extra arguments in endif()".
Why does catkin_lint complain about that - even if it's just a notice?
It is standard practice to add the same expression as in the if-block there, if the block
exceeds a certain length. This is to make it more easy to understand the logical structure
of the build script.

In my opinion this notice should be removed again, as it's just noise among more helpful notices like
"list ... should be sorted" or "operands for operator VERSION_LESS should be quoted strings" (same project...)

cmake's help states:
> Note that the expression in the else and endif clause is optional.

This is something entirely different from "the use is discouraged" (which is what a lint notice usually implies)

issue_title: Do not crash when asked for a parameter after end date
issue_body:
As someone who use openfisca as a backend of a simulation application,
My application doesn't crash overnight without me having done anything,
Because we just passed the end date of one legislative parameter

----------------

The changes introduced with the fuzzy parameters are a catastrophe for anyone using openfisca in prod. It introduces a major production risk : if we pass the end date of a parameter we use, then the application cannot do any calculation anymore.

The PNDS (institutional re-user) application was down for some time because of this, as well as some of our testing instances.

Openfisca should offer a "production" mode where it is flexible and extrapolates parameters values, and a "strict" mode where it crashed when a value is missing if it is necessary.

issue_title: Suggestion: check 'unique together' after form submission
issue_body:
mass-edit correctly excludes unique fields from the form before it even gets created.  What would be very helpful to prevent potential problems is to add a check to the form after its filled in (but before the change process is run) so that the 'unique_together' property in the model Meta is also validated.  

For example, I have a Location type of model.  Neither its 'name' or 'region' are individually unique, but the combination has to be.  The mass-edit form should throw an error if a user tries to fill in both of those fields at the same time.

issue_title: Sparse update for embedding matrix technically incorrect for momentum (but probably better)
issue_body:
Hi,

I noticed this issue in my own implementation, and remembered that your solvers also use a sparse update implementation for the embedding matrix. I thought I'd drop you a note.

The issue is that most momentum-based solvers involve some sort of exponential decay on the momentum vector. In my implementation, this decay was being applied unevenly, because the solver would only be called for words present in the minibatch. If I'm reading your implementation right, I think the same is true for you.

The technically correct solution is pretty obvious --- we can just track a timestamp, and do the update lazily as `momentum ** (current_time - time_of_last_update)`.

However, in practice I think this solution might be worse than the "bug". It would mean rare words get no momentum, because we can expect them to have a long time between updates. Alternatively we could set the momentum decay really low, which would make the gradients of the rare words too sticky.

For the classical momentum, the "bug" version means that the momentum is simply the EMA of the _active_ gradients for that word. I'm satisfied with this.

However, I think there might be some problem for other solvers, such as Adam, which also take the number of overall updates as a parameter. We might consider using the number of updates for the feature, instead. In my implementation, I happen to have the timestamp of the last change to the feature, and I didn't want to track another book keeping variable for a minor thing. So I'm guessing the number of updates using the time since last change, `time / (time-last_upd)`.

I doubt that there's a huge difference in results from this issue, either way. But it might be good to note ways in which the implementation behaves differently from others. A similar situation occurs for regularization penalties. I saw from another issue that you're aware of that.

Best,
Matt

_rsl_interface._label_volume(rsl_volume, radar)

file = '/Volumes/42/CHILL/ORIG/20160717/CHL20160717_222347.uf'

Next, I have a simple MapReduce job that counts the number of items of each type. The Mapper emits (type, 1). The reducer simply adds the number of 1s received for each type.

When I run this job with 5000 shards, the map-stage runs fine. It uses a total of 20 instances which is maximum possible based on my task-queue configuration.

However, the shuffle-hash stage makes use of only one instance and fails with an out-of-memory error. I am not able to understand why only one instance is being used for hashing and how can I fix this out-of-memory error in /mapreduce/kickoffjob_callback/15714734************.

I have tried writing a combiner but I never saw a combiner stage on the mapreduce status page or in the logs.

issue_title: Cloud Storage: Overflow error: Python int too large to convert to C long from blob.py
issue_body:
# Thank you for reporting an issue to google-cloud-python!

If you are reporting an issue or requesting a feature, please search the
existing open and closed issues to see if there is already work being done.

- https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues
- http://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/google-cloud-python

If you can provide us with as much of the following information as possible it
will help us identify the cause of your issue more quickly.

1. Specify the API at the beginning of the title (for example, "BigQuery: ...")
   General, Core, and Other are also allowed as types

2. OS type and version 
Microsoft Windows [Version 6.1.7601]

3. Python version and virtual environment information `python --version`
2.7.13

4. google-cloud-python version `pip show google-cloud`, `pip show google-<service>` or `pip freeze`

google-cloud-storage==1.7.0

5. Stacktrace if available

`Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:/Dev/Python/widdler/widdler.py", line 605, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "C:/Dev/Python/widdler/widdler.py", line 593, in main
    result = args.func(args)
  File "C:/Dev/Python/widdler/widdler.py", line 408, in call_upload
    uploaded_files = b.upload_workflow_input_files(args.wdl, args.json)
  File "C:\Dev\Python\widdler\src\SingleBucket.py", line 189, in upload_workflow_input_files
    self.upload_files(files_to_upload)
  File "C:\Dev\Python\widdler\src\SingleBucket.py", line 107, in upload_files
    self.upload_file(f, f.replace('\\', '/'))
  File "C:\Dev\Python\widdler\src\SingleBucket.py", line 91, in upload_file
    blob.upload_from_filename(source_file_name)
  File "C:\Miniconda3\envs\Widdler\lib\site-packages\google\cloud\storage\blob.py", line 992, in upload_from_filename
    size=total_bytes)
  File "C:\Miniconda3\envs\Widdler\lib\site-packages\google\cloud\storage\blob.py", line 946, in upload_from_file
    client, file_obj, content_type, size, num_retries)
  File "C:\Miniconda3\envs\Widdler\lib\site-packages\google\cloud\storage\blob.py", line 867, in _do_upload
    client, stream, content_type, size, num_retries)
  File "C:\Miniconda3\envs\Widdler\lib\site-packages\google\cloud\storage\blob.py", line 676, in _do_multipart_upload
    data = stream.read(size)
OverflowError: Python int too large to convert to C long`

6. Steps to reproduce

Use blob.upload_from_filename(source_file_name) to upload a file that's 3.8GB in size. Guessing anything
larger than 2.1 GB will do as this appears to be an issue of the file size being handled as an int when it should be handled as a long. 

7. Code example

This is my function:
`
    def upload_file(self, source_file_name, destination_blob_name):
        """
        Upload a file to a bucket. Once they are in the bucket, they are called blobs.
        :param source_file_name: Full path and name of source file.
        :param destination_blob_name: name to give file in bucket.
        :return:
        """
        blob = self.bucket.blob(destination_blob_name)

        try:
            blob.upload_from_filename(source_file_name)
        except Exception as e:
            print_log_exit(str(e))
`
It is part of a class I made for implementing various cloud storage functionality for a single bucket. When attempting to upload a large file (3.8GB in size), I get the overflow error in the stack trace above. While tracking it down in debugger, the error appears to be caused by line 676 of blob.py in the google cloud storage library:

`            data = stream.read(size)
`

issue_title: projects.models.versions.setDefault() requires empty body parameter
issue_body:
When calling [projects.models.versions.setDefault](https://cloud.google.com/ml-engine/reference/rest/v1/projects.models.versions/setDefault):
```
credentials = GoogleCredentials.get_application_default()
ml = discovery.build('ml', 'v1', credentials=credentials)
ml.projects().models().versions().setDefault(
        name=model_version,
        body={} # Must be set, even if empty.
    )
```
It seems that body must be passed even if it is empty. In other methods, body is simply omitted if it is not required, but in setDefault, omitting the body parameter gives an error:
```
/Projects/pythonenv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/googleapiclient/discovery.py in method(self, **kwargs)
    734         if name not in _PAGE_TOKEN_NAMES or _findPageTokenName(

--> 736           raise TypeError('Missing required parameter "%s"' % name)
    737 
    738     for name, regex in six.iteritems(parameters.pattern_params):

TypeError: Missing required parameter "body"
```

issue_body:
我不清楚是shadowsocks还是SwitchyOmega的问题，所以向两位开发者都提交这个bug。

shadowsocks-dns远程解析bug，翻墙网址会向本地dns提交dns解析，本地dns会返回错误的ip，从而会记录谁几时登陆了某网址！
试验环境，
1，A机器xp，原版，
2，安装有wireshark，截获dns数据
3，本地设置114.114.114.114dns
4，chrome浏览器，安装SwitchyOmega，设置127.0.0.1:65500的socks5代理
5，shadowsocks代理。
截获步骤：
一、
1，无论设置SwitchyOmega自动代理，gfwed，登陆youtube.com，youtube可以打开，说明选择了正确的远程dns解析ip
2，wireshark，抓取数据包。
3，wireshark，分析中包含，有114返回的错误ip
![123](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/10794158/5991448/3b712acc-aa2b-11e4-9299-921960c241c1.jpg)

结论：说明浏览器向远程ip和本地dns都发送了dns解析请求，但是只选择了远程的dns解析ip，但是同时本地的114上会有，某时间点本机所在外网ip请求了youtube。com的记录。如果倒查就爆菊了。

如果某人不想让某人知道自己上了某网址。那么这样就无法实现。
如果不想发送本地dns请求就要将dns设置成空，那么就不会去本地发送dns解析。但是问题又来了，如果在自动模式下，未翻墙网址就打不开了。

综上所述：
我的意思就是说能不能设置成翻墙dns解析走远程ip，强制不发送dns本地解析。谢谢开发者！

Here is an example that should not pass validation:

```
import troposphere.ec2 as ec2

ec2.SecurityGroupRule(
    IpProtocol="tcp",
    FromPort="22",
    ToPort="22",
    CidrIp="0.0.0.0/0",
    SourceSecurityGroupId=sg-12345
)
```

issue_title: Create test_infra around pants_run invocations.
issue_body:
It would be nice to provide a stable API for querying the integration tests that call shelled pants runs. In particular, perhaps return a class object that has attributes that know:
  * goal invocations
  * goal order
  * targets processed and target order
  * etc.

Essentially return an object that contains the basic info about the run, akin to what you could see by watching the stdout from a -`linfo` pants run.

The are a bunch of tests that sketch out this functionality, and in a variety of ways. Providing an API would give us more modular and intuitive code. It might also lead to more robust tests due to the framework making the additional test signal easy to get.

Some tests that implement their own logic for this functionality include:

      contrib/android/tests/python/pants_test/contrib/android/tasks/test_aapt_gen_integration.py
      tests/python/pants_test/backend/codegen/tasks/test_protobuf_integration.py
      tests/python/pants_test/core_tasks/test_prep_command_integration.py

and I am sure more.

issue_title: Add a flag to enable use of `LegacyBuildGraph` without pantsd.
issue_body:
currently, to use the new engine-backed `LegacyBuildGraph` the daemon must be enabled and running. the scope of this task would be to add a flag (e.g. `--enable-new-graph`) which would cause pants to construct and use the `LegacyBuildGraph` for a non-daemon run.

this will enable more reliable integration testing against the new `BuildGraph` facade - sans any actual resident caching that the daemon provides.

issue_title: Jar dependencies from a jvm_tool collide with main classpath
issue_body:
If a jvm tool has transitive dependency on a jar with newer version then in 3rdparty then Ivy will resolve only the newest one.

For a real life example see description of https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2253.

issue_title: CodeUri intrinsic function doesn't work for S3 bucket
issue_body:
I defined CodeUri param with !Sub function in YAML:

```
Parameters:
  Version:
     Type: String
     Default: latest
     Description: Lambda version.
MyFunc:
      Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
      Properties:
        FunctionName: MyFuncName
        CodeUri: !Sub "s3://my_backet/my_app/${Version}/code.zip"
        Handler: func.handler
        Role: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/RoleName"
```

And only Specifying CodeUri using inartistic functions caused exception. 

1. Define "CodeUri" with  intrinsic function: `CodeUri: !Sub "s3://my_backet/my_app/${Version}/code.zip"`
2. run 'sam deploy --template-file template.yaml --stack-name stack_name'


**Observed result:**
Exception:
`Failed to create the changeset: Waiter ChangeSetCreateComplete failed: Waiter encountered a terminal failure state Status: FAILED. Reason: Transform AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31 failed with: Invalid Serverless Application Specification document. Number of errors found: 1. Resource with id [MyFunc] is invalid. 'CodeUri' requires Bucket and Key properties to be specified`
**Expected result:**
CodeUri could by defined as S3 bucket using inartistic functions like !Sub

issue_title: Create and document a more systematic automated testing procedure
issue_body:
While writing tests in https://github.com/oduwsdl/ipwb/commit/1bf14a3f48b584a5300cc9e9ec0e592a9811552f, I could not wield my local Python, Pip, IPFS, and IPWB into a consistent environment to obtain the same results as TravisCI reported in PRs. 

With @ibnesayeed's help, I was able to get replicable testing using our current Docker build and configuration but the commands needed to perform this operation along with setting up the environment (e.g., installing requirements per the .travis.yml) are things I will forget.

@ibnesayeed mentioned a Dockerfile could also be created for testing. Let's do and document this to replicate the TravisCI & pytest tests and have a more consistent, replicable test-running environment independent of the CI system.

`docker container run --rm -it -v "$PWD":/ipwb -p 5000:5000 oduwsdl/ipwb bash`

Once in container:

```
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -r test-requirements.txt
pip install pycodestyle
pip install codecov
pip install pytest-cov
pycodestyle
ipfs init
ipfs daemon & sleep 10
py.test --cov=./
```

issue_title: Pushing an empty UrlList has no effect
issue_body:
We are currently migrating our product to the new destination URLs and are hitting a snag. Setting finalUrls and finalMobilUrls on regular keywords works fine, but after setting URLs it is impossible to clear the list. This is the dictionary that we are passing to the mutate method:

    {'operand': {'adGroupId': '<redacted>',
              'criterion': {'id': '<redacted>',
                            'matchType': u'EXACT',
                            'text': u'some keyword',
                            'xsi_type': 'Keyword'},
              'destinationUrl': '',
              'finalMobileUrls': {'urls': []},
              'finalUrls': {'urls': []},
              'xsi_type': 'BiddableAdGroupCriterion'},
  'operator': 'SET'} 

The following XML blob is created by suds:

    <?xml version="1.0" ?>
    <SOAP-ENV:Envelope xmlns:SOAP-ENV="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:ns0="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:ns1="https://adwords.google.com/api/adwords/cm/v201502" xmlns:tns="https://adwords.google.com/api/adwords/cm/v201502" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
        <SOAP-ENV:Header>
            <tns:RequestHeader>
                <tns:clientCustomerId>REDACTED</tns:clientCustomerId>
                <tns:developerToken>REDACTED</tns:developerToken>
                <tns:userAgent>(AwApi-Python, googleads/3.4.1, Python/2.7)</tns:userAgent>
                <tns:validateOnly>false</tns:validateOnly>
                <tns:partialFailure>false</tns:partialFailure>
            </tns:RequestHeader>
        </SOAP-ENV:Header>
        <ns0:Body>
            <ns1:mutate>
                <ns1:operations>
                    <ns1:operator>SET</ns1:operator>
                    <ns1:operand xsi:type="ns1:BiddableAdGroupCriterion">
                        <ns1:adGroupId>REDACTED</ns1:adGroupId>
                        <ns1:criterion xsi:type="ns1:Keyword">
                            <ns1:id>REDACTED</ns1:id>
                            <ns1:Criterion.Type>Keyword</ns1:Criterion.Type>
                            <ns1:text>some keyword</ns1:text>
                            <ns1:matchType>EXACT</ns1:matchType>
                        </ns1:criterion>
                        <ns1:AdGroupCriterion.Type>BiddableAdGroupCriterion</ns1:AdGroupCriterion.Type>
                        <ns1:destinationUrl/>
                    </ns1:operand>
                </ns1:operations>
            </ns1:mutate>
        </ns0:Body>
    </SOAP-ENV:Envelope>


In this XML blob the new (empty) UrlLists for finalUrls and finalMobileUrls are missing, and hence they are not cleared in AdWords. Setting the urls key to None or the finalUrls to {} or None doesn't solve the problem. It appears that either the adwords client or suds is removing the empty object.

Edit: this is using the latest versions of suds and googleads on Python 2.7.

issue_title: Replace Ansible template with reactive template
issue_body:
## Checklist

 - [X] Confirmed this is an issue with charm-tools, not charmstore-client



## Issue/Feature

The shipping ansible template uses a classic hook paradigm approach to delivering charms with ansible. There was work to make a [reactive base layer](https://github.com/chuckbutler/ansible-base), and to extend ansible support into the new paradigm. Making integration efforts and sharing easier. We should evaluate this work to replace the current Ansible template.

I start nginx, then exabgp service. No issue, route is announced to backbone. When i stop nginx (make some test), exabgp crash (due to an exception on healthcheck). When i run exabgp without systemd (manualy), no issue. Issued appear only when exabgp is started as service.

issue_title: Pretty Tables
BaseEstimator.state_counts() erroneously reports zero for nodes with a single parent
issue_body:
### Subject of the issue
When computing state counts for nodes with a single parent, the function `BaseEstimator.state_counts()` erroneously reports zero.

### Your environment
* pgmpy version: commit [f9be170]; also reproduced in 0.1.6
* Python version: Python 3.6.4
* Operating System: macOS High Sierra (10.13.3)

### Steps to reproduce
```python
import pandas as pd

from pgmpy.estimators import BaseEstimator

data = pd.DataFrame(data={'fruit': ["banana", "apple", "banana", "apple", "banana","apple", "banana", 
                                    "apple", "apple", "apple", "banana", "banana", "apple", "banana",], 
                          'tasty': ["yes", "no", "yes", "yes", "yes", "yes", "yes", 
                                    "yes", "yes", "yes", "yes", "no", "no", "no"], 
                          'size': ["large", "large", "large", "small", "large", "large", "large",
                                    "small", "large", "large", "large", "large", "small", "small"]})

be = BaseEstimator(data)
print(be.state_counts('tasty', parents=['fruit']))  # conditional on fruit
```

### Expected behaviour
```
fruit  apple  banana
tasty               
no         2       2
yes        5       5
```

### Actual behaviour
```
 fruit apple banana
tasty             
no      0.0    0.0
yes     0.0    0.0
```

issue_title: Flaws in Bayesian parameter estimation
issue_body:
I wanted to do Bayesian parameter estimation for Bayesian networks. I found that section in your book in  chapter 5. But it seems there are some flaws in it.
1.) I do not find a way to specify priors i.e. the initial counts for discrete nodes or mu, sigma for continuous nodes. Please help.

2.) Another trivial one is that if we give too few training examples, your system gives an error.  
E.g. Running the example given in your book page -182 with only 10 data samples- I got this error
ValueError: operands could not be broadcast together with shapes (5,) (8,) 

Ideally, if we do not find the data for the values a node (suppose 'C') takes, given some values of its parents (suppose for A =1 and B=2 does not appear in the data at all ), it should set the probabilities for all the values of that node, given that parent configuration to be equal. ( e.g. P(A = 1| A =1 and B=2 ) =  P(A = 1| A =1 and B=2 ) = 1/2 ). I would be glad if you can include that in your script.

3.) Also wanted to know If your library support hybrid (discrete+ continuous) bayesian networks? Specially parameter learning and inferencing in such networks.

issue_title: Incorrect generated library.py for arrays of ViReal64
nifgen metadata issues
issue_body:
There are methods and properties that aren't public, yet we have metadata for them. This is most likely historical.

Example:
```
    'ExportSignal':                             { 'parameters': { 1: { 'enum': 'Signal',                    }, }, },
    'SetNamedWaveformNextWritePosition':        { 'parameters': { 3: { 'enum': 'RelativeTo',                }, }, },
    'SetWaveformNextWritePosition':             { 'parameters': { 3: { 'enum': 'RelativeTo',                }, }, },
```

Metadata for `WaitUntilDone` is wrong. Should be a timedelta, not a timeout of 10,000 seconds:
```
'WaitUntilDone':                                { 'parameters': { 1: { 'default_value': 10000, }, }, },
```

In order to fix minor documentation issues, this needs to specify `'public_name_for_documentation'`:
```
    'InitiateGeneration':                   { 'codegen_method': 'private',  },
```

`create_advanced_arb_sequence` is a superset of `create_arb_sequence`. There are reasons why we have both in C (historical and IVI compliance) but in Python we can remove the code-generated `create_arb_sequence` and rename `create_advanced_arb_sequence`.

issue_title: asyncio/websocket.py not compatible with Python 3.7 syntax changes
issue_body:
This change in Python 3.7:

> Backwards incompatible syntax changes:
> 
> async and await are now reserved keywords.

Broke this code block in asyncio/websocket.py:
```
if hasattr(asyncio, 'ensure_future'):
    ensure_future = asyncio.ensure_future
else:  # Deprecated since Python 3.4.4
    ensure_future = asyncio.async
```

Example failure log:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
 File REDACTED, line 18, in <module>
   from autobahn.asyncio.websocket import WebSocketServerFactory, WebSocketServerProtocol
 File “/usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/autobahn/asyncio/__init__.py”, line 36, in <module>
   from autobahn.asyncio.websocket import \
 File “/usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/autobahn/asyncio/websocket.py”, line 52
   ensure_future = asyncio.async
                               ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax
```


I fixed the issue locally by just deleting the old import, but I don't know what preferred fix in autobahn official library is.  It would probably work to use getattr(asyncio, "async") to preserve backwards compatibility while still respecting the new Python 3.7 syntax rules.

issue_title: PortStat - Counter not available in 2.6
issue_body:
`from collections import Counter` from [here](https://github.com/python-diamond/Diamond/blob/master/src/collectors/portstat/portstat.py#L10) does not work in python 2.6 as it was implemented in 2.7

A backported version of Counter can be located [here](http://code.activestate.com/recipes/576611-counter-class/).  It might be good to include this somewhere in the project as a fallback in case the collections.Counter can't be loaded.

issue_body:
Seems that `Collector.get_metric_path` is guilty. Last return in this method `return '.'.join([prefix, path, name])` uses prefix which is blank in these cases. Sample metrics from StatsD's logs:

But we should change the behavior to throw an error instead. It is a naming convention issue, and our consensus is to avoid it.

integration tests should run against existing cluster

issue_title: ref(controller): API views need refactoring
issue_body: With Django Rest Framework v3, a lot of our Class views can be optimized, refactored, and otherwise hardened such that we no longer need to update function calls in multiple classes. This will really help us with future security patches, such as the work being done at #2853.

issue_title: store: Improve performance by setting NOCOW on the mounted volume
issue_body:
Disabling [copy-on-write](https://coreos.com/docs/cluster-management/debugging/btrfs-troubleshooting/#disable-copy-on-write) for Ceph seems to be a good idea.

I'm wondering, though, if we'll need to do this once CoreOS switches over to OverlayFS instead of btrfs for `/var/lib/docker`, but it's probably worth exploring before then.

refs #2647
refs #2927

issue_title: git push hangs on Azure when deploying an app if it takes > ~10 min
issue_body:
Hi all

I'm testing deis on Microsoft Azure.
The first time I create and deploy an app it usually takes > 10 min.
For example, when I create and deploy deis/example-php it takes ~15 min (in this case controller, builder and publisher are Standard_D1 VMs).
Deployment is successful, however git push always hangs, in my case at "-----> Pushing image to private registry".
In Azure you can set loadbalancer idle timeout and even public ip idle timeout as high as 30 min (see https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/documentation/articles/virtual-networks-load-balancer-manage-idle-timeout/ ); however this is not solving the problem.
So I've done some tests.
From outside the cluster, git push hangs pushing both at port 2222 and 2223 (first case reaching nginx, second reaching the builder directly).
From inside the cluster, after creating an app, I changed the remote branch url and set it to the local address of the builder (at port 2223) or nginx (at port 2222) and then git push just works without hanging. 
From inside the cluster pushing at the public ip hangs in both cases. 

When sniffing with Wireshark the connection seems to be alive; it is not closed, however no traffic is generated until I CTRL-C git push and the connection is obviously closed.

Can you please help me found out what the problem is?
Isn't it worth to implement some sort of application level keep alive, and possibly have more precise progress status?
Possibly related: #3755.
Can you also post some benchmark or estimated time it takes to push deis sample apps in various aws instances so I can compare?

Thanks

issue_title: bin/compile in buildpacks are not being passed $3 (ENV_DIR)
issue_body:
While working through some layering issues with http://github.com/ianblenke/rbenv-buildpack, it seemed a bit out of place that deis does not appear to pass the environment directory argument to the compile script for a buildpack.

With this in my bin/compile script:
```
BUILD_DIR=$1 # The app directory, usually /app. This will have the app source initially. Whatever is left here will be persisted.
CACHE_DIR=$2 # The contents of CACHE_DIR will be persisted between builds so we can use it to speed the builds up
ENV_DIR=$3     # An envdir directory of the app's environment variables

echo BUILD_DIR="$BUILD_DIR"
echo CACHE_DIR="$CACHE_DIR"
echo ENV_DIR="$ENV_DIR"
```
Applying it as a buildpack to a deis deployed project will output this:
```
BUILD_DIR=/tmp/build
CACHE_DIR=/tmp/cache
ENV_DIR=
```

Oddly, there is no third argument for the environment directory, as mentioned in the https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/buildpack-api

Is this by design? As environment variables are natively passed and used by default, is this feature deprecated? I may be missing some heroku lore here that would explain this documentation/implementation divergence.

issue_title: Update 'Using the CLI' file transfer example to non-deprecated style
issue_body:
The transfer command example in the docs (https://docs.globus.org/cli/using-the-cli/#file_transfer) fails with message:

```
$ ssh ****@cli.globusonline.org transfer -- go#ep1:/share/godata/file1.txt go#ep2:~/myfile.txt
Error: Invalid endpoint name 'go#ep1:': Invalid characters
```

I had to play with other commands to find a solution. The ls command returns a deprecation warning when the SCP syntax is used:

```
$ ssh ****@cli.globusonline.org ls go#ep1:/share/godata/
Warning: SCP style (endpoint:path) URLs are deprecated
Warning: Use endpoint/path or endpoint/~/path instead
```

Probably easier to update the docs, but a similar warning message might be ideal.

issue_title: Implement pattern/filter support in add_item(s) transfer function
issue_body:
Currently (based off the documentation) it appears that the add_item() function only allows you to add a single item to either transfer or delete at one time via the "/source/path/dir/", "/dest/path/dir/" arguments (and also to recursively transfer files).
In my application, It would be very handy to be able to use a pattern or filter in order to add all items that match the pattern or filter. The behaviour I'm looking for would essentially replace the following code with just one add_item call with the pattern, and then the transfer (would still need to generate my pattern):

```
listing_pattern = "name:~*{}*rawacf.bz2".format(self.sync_radar) # You absolutely need the '~' for this pattern to work. Not obvious from docs
listing = self.transfer_client.operation_ls(source_uuid,path=listing_path,filter=listing_pattern)
files_to_sync = [entry['name'] for entry in listing]
transfer_data = globus_sdk.TransferData(self.transfer_client, source_uuid, dest_uuid, label=inspect.currentframe().f_code.co_name,sync_level="checksum")
source_dir_prefix = "/chroot/sddata/raw/{}/{}/".format(self.sync_year,self.sync_month)
dest_dir_prefix = self.sync_local_dir
for file in files_to_sync:
    transfer_data.add_item("{}/{}".format(source_dir_prefix,file),"{}/{}".format(dest_dir_prefix,file))
transfer_result = self.transfer_client.submit_transfer(transfer_data)
return transfer_result
```

issue_title: typify for str + NoneType is wrong
issue_body:
https://github.com/conda/conda/pull/5107#issuecomment-296356913

It still seems to me as if `typify()` is doing something wrong:
```python
>>> from conda._vendor.auxlib.configuration import typify
>>> typify('false', str)
'false'
>>> typify('false', (str, type(None)))
'False'
```
Why should addition of type `None` introduce such a change? The code flow is:

```python
elif not (type_hint - (STRING_TYPES_SET | {NoneType})):
    value = typify_str_no_hint(text_type(value))
    return None if value is None else text_type(value)
```

I wonder why not just return `text_type(value)`? Why call `typify_str_no_hint`?

issue_title: Mixing and matching python 2 and python 3 environments with separate modules
issue_body:
I've been working with a python 3.6.0 installation set up with Ananconda 4.3.0:
```python
Python 3.6.0 |Anaconda 4.3.0 (64-bit)| (default, Dec 23 2016, 12:22:00) 
[GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-1)] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
```
I'm collaborating on a project that is written in python 2.x and I'd like to use all the `conda` tools, so I created a new python 2.7 environment for the project following the guidelines [here](https://conda.io/docs/using/envs.html) and [here](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/30492623/using-both-python-2-x-and-python-3-x-in-ipython-notebook/30492913#30492913). Specifically:
```bash
conda create -n py27 python=2.7 ipykernel
source activate py27
```
I can confirm the environments are setup correctly:
```bash
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] 1 $ source activate py27
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [py27] 2s $ python --version
Python 2.7.13 :: Continuum Analytics, Inc.
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [py27] $ source activate root
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [root] 2s $ python --version
Python 3.6.0 :: Anaconda 4.3.0 (64-bit)
```

While in the `py27` environment, I run `conda install yaml` to install the `yaml` module for that environment and I'm told it's already installed (and the path looks right to me, showing `py27`):
```bash
# All requested packages already installed.
# packages in environment at /home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27:
#
yaml                      0.1.6                         0  
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [py27] 4s $ 
```
But if I launch `python` in the `py27` environment, I can't load the module, even though the module is installed in (seemingly) the right place:
```python
[dslochower:~/data … anaconda3/envs/py27] [py27] 1 $ python -c "import sys; print sys.version; print sys.path; import yaml"
2.7.13 |Continuum Analytics, Inc.| (default, Dec 20 2016, 23:09:15) 
[GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-1)]
['', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python27.zip', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/plat-linux2', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/lib-tk', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/lib-old', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/lib-dynload', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages', '/home/dslochower/data/applications/anaconda3/envs/py27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/setuptools-27.2.0-py2.7.egg']
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
ImportError: No module named yaml
```
It does work, however, if I call `python3` from within the `py27` environment and load `yaml`. It appears to me that the `yaml` module is installed in the `py27` environment, but only for python 3.x. Is there a way to force `conda` to install this module for python 2.x? Am I misunderstanding how the modules go along with the environments?  As far as I can tell, from running `conda list` in either environment, I have `yaml` version 0.1.6 installed, both with "build string" set to "0".

issue_title: Conda-build: Remove dependency on setuptools and a setup.py for python packages. (redundant meta.yaml)
issue_body:
All the information is in the meta.yaml.  Why not do away with setuptools and setup.py?

Build.sh can already replace setup.py by:
- Extracting tarball into site-packages
- Add package to conda.pth
- Add entrypoints to bin/
- ... etc

... so why have so much information duplicated in both meta.yaml and setup.py, requiring manual transcription?

(If it's absolutely necessary, why not have a conda-build step that generates one from the other)

issue_title: Odd behavior when specifying a version
issue_body:
- conda 4.0.7
- OSX

`conda install nodejs`   - get the familiar `Fetching package metadata: ....
Solving package specifications: .........` etc. great :D

`conda install -c javascript nodejs` get the familiar `Fetching package metadata: ....
Solving package specifications: .........`

All good so far. But specifying a version is not good.

`conda install nodejs>=4.1` returns no feedback just spits me onto a new line

`conda install -c javascript nodejs>=4.1` returns no feedback but stalls and I'm just left waiting for my prompt to return

issue_title: pip writes into a cache that is not environment specific leading to problems when attempting to rebuild.
issue_body:
to reproduce have an environment that uses a pip package that depends on a shared library.
```
conda env create -f environment.yml
conda env remove -n {env}
mv lib{foo.so.5.0.0,foo.so.5.0.1}
ln -s libfoo.so libfoo.so.5.0.1
conda env create -f environment.yml 
```

this will fail due to the pip cache in the user's home directory referencing the old shared library.
conda clean --all doesn't fix this because it doesn't touch the pip cache.
conda needs to move the pip cache into the environment directory when installing pip packages so that a clean, or remove will properly remove said references.

issue_title: Improve conda search
issue_body:
@ahmadia identified problems with current search results in https://github.com/Anaconda-Server/anaconda-server/issues/710.  I believe we need to improve the results by:
- we need to prioritize exact matches first
- then we need to use some distance metric factored in to the remaining order

I have also created an issue for this on the Anaconda Server side (which impacts the web GUI and the CLI client): https://github.com/Anaconda-Server/anaconda-server/issues/919

issue_title: openblas not built with full processor support
issue_body:
The openblas library appears to have been built only for the Nehalem processor. However, openblas has runtime cpu detection, and so it should be built with support for all processors.

Also, it's not clear which version of the library you are shipping - is it the sequential library, a pthreads library, or an OpenMP library? It would be good to have all three.

You can have a look at how it's supposed to be built at the Fedora spec file
 https://src.fedoraproject.org/cgit/rpms/openblas.git/tree/openblas.spec

For the dynamic architecture support, the flags you need are e.g.
TARGET=CORE2 DYNAMIC_ARCH=1

issue_title: VariantHeader__str__ returns non-ascii characters
issue_body:
Calling str(VariantFile.header) returns something that looks like binary noise. Looking at the code, I think this is a simple bug:

    def __str__(self):
        cdef int hlen
        cdef char *hstr = bcf_hdr_fmt_text(self.ptr, 0, &hlen)

        ret = hstr[:hlen]
        free(hstr)
        return force_str(hstr)

So *hstr* is freed and *ret* is ignored.

issue_title: release  wheel package to pypi server
issue_body:
hi, thanks for your pysam, it's easy to use, and very powerful library. but `pip install pysam` is too slow, may be your should release binary format package to pypi server.

you could release wheel format package as following:

```bash
python setup.py bdist_wheel
```

BTY, you could use trivis's `deploy` feature which it automatic release package and push to pypi server. for ref: https://github.com/Liuchang0812/OSSPy/blob/master/.travis.yml

issue_title: default value for info.END in VariantRecord
issue_body:
Hi,
I try to use pysam to generate Vcf files and it seems to be a default value for the END tag of info field. It is not mandatory according to spec (and no use for SNP i think).  
Is there a way to make it disappear from the output ?

```
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

import pysam

o_vcf = pysam.VariantFile( "out.vcf", 'w' )
o_vcf.header.contigs.add( 'A02' )

# ----------------
d_var = {
		# start is 0-based
		'contig' : 'A02', 'start': 9,
	}
o_rec = o_vcf.new_record( **d_var )
o_vcf.write( o_rec )
# A02	10	.	.	.	.	.	.

# ----------------
d_var = {
		# start is 0-based
		'contig' : 'A02', 'start': 9,
		'alleles': [ 'T', 'C' ],
	}
o_rec = o_vcf.new_record( **d_var )
o_vcf.write( o_rec )
# A02	10	.	T	C	.	.	END=0

```

issue_title: RPM name conflict
issue_body:
I create a RPM file according the Python [manual](https://docs.python.org/2/distutils/builtdist.html#creating-rpm-packages): 
```
python setup.py bdist --formats=rpm
rpmbuild -bb build/bdist.linux-x86_64/rpm/SPECS/elasticsearch.spec
```
This command generate a RPM specification file which contains the same name like the 'real' Elasticsarch [RPM](https://artifacts.elastic.co/downloads/elasticsearch/elasticsearch-5.3.0.rpm) and cause a conflict when you install both RPM files.

**Solution**
- Change the name in setup.py to 'python-elasticsearch'

issue_title: Include a simple way of getting into a development environment
issue_body:
In my projects, I include a `devenv.sh` script that uses a docker container and a shared volume including the source directory that makes it incredibly painless for new users to make contributions.

If the `RUN` commands in the `Dockerfile` were externalized into a `./build.sh` script, then the `Dockerfile` could be used to the build the development container, and can still be used to do point in time builds of source code by running it with `docker build -t az-devenv .; docker run -v $(pwd):/az-cli -it az-devenv bash ./build.sh`.

That will allow the actual enlistment to override the source that was added with `ADD` in the `Dockerfile`.

Doing this would allow me to type `./devenv.sh` and get a fully working development environment, instead of spending a non-trivial amount of time trying to get Python and pythonpath and whatnot to be configured correctly.

issue_body:

```
Command

        The generator will lazily follow the continuation tokens returned by the service and stop

        specified and the account has more than that number of  containers, the generator will have
        a populated next_marker field once it  finishes. This marker can be used to create a new
        generator if more  results are desired.
```

issue_title: Table output supresses IDs and Types but in some cases, it would be beneficial
issue_body:
Table output hides IDs and types by default as ids aren't table-friendly and types are typically not needed. e.g. az vm list -otable would show Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines in each row of the table.
However, there are special cases (e.g. Storage data-plane commands and `az resource show -otable`) where showing IDs/types in table output would be useful.

A workaround is to create a custom callable table format for those specific commands that need to display IDs or Types.

Related to https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/1003 and https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/1039.

issue_title: [DNS] Help for 'az network dns record-set delete' refers to zone instead of record set
issue_body:
Consider:

```
C:\Users\jonatul>az network dns record-set delete -h

Command
    az network dns record-set delete: Deletes a record set within a DNS zone.

Arguments
    --name -n           [Required]: The name of the record set, relative to the name of the zone.
    --resource-group -g [Required]: Name of resource group.
    --type -t           [Required]: The type of DNS records in the record set.  Allowed values: A,
                                    AAAA, CNAME, MX, NS, PTR, SRV, TXT.
    --zone-name -z      [Required]: The name of the zone.
    --if-match                    : Defines the If-Match condition. The delete operation will be
                                    performed only if the ETag of the zone on the server matches
                                    this value.
    --if-none-match               : Defines the If-None-Match condition. The delete operation will
                                    be performed only if the ETag of the zone on the server does not
                                    match this value.
```

The references above to 'zone' under `--if-match` and `--if-none-match` should actually refer to record sets (looks like a copy + paste error).

For '--if-match', please can we simply use the text directly from the [DNS Swagger](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/master/arm-dns/2016-04-01/swagger/dns.json)? I think it is clearer: "The etag of the record set. Omit this value to always delete the current record set. Specify the last-seen etag value to prevent accidentally deleting any concurrent changes."

issue_title: Ability to Configure Azure CLI Trusted Root CAs
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

For an Azure Stack install, there is a requirement to add a Root CA for access via the Azure CLI. However, it is not currently possible to configure a custom CA for the Azure CLI toolset, and the Azure Stack documentation requires you to manually update the CA list in the library used by the Azure CLI toolset. Replacing the CA list in the library is problematic, as upgrading Azure CLI toolset will wipe out the trusted CA list. 

https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-stack/user/azure-stack-version-profiles-azurecli2

**Describe the solution you'd like**

It would be ideal to have a version-neutral configuration for adding trusted root CAs. Having a trusted CA directory under `$AZURE_CONFIG_DIR/` would be best.

issue_title: VM Access: command renaming
issue_body:
This is polishing like change regarding 4 existing commands under `vm access`, including `delete-linux-user`, `reset-linux-ssh`, `reset-windows-admin`, `set-linux-user`. The naming oddness reflects the service function difference between windows and linux vm, particularly on windows vm, you will not be able to add/rename/delete users, even though the API invoke always succeeds but nothing really happens at vm side
The change details are:
1. Rename category `vm access` to `vm user`
2. Collapse into 3 commands
    `delete-user`: delete a user from linux vm
    `update-user`: for windows vm, you can only update the admin password, for linux vm, you can add, or change both username and password, also you can upload public ssh key
    `reset-ssh`: reset linux vm’s SSH configurations

issue_title: az acr build times out quicker than expected in some clients
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**

`az acr build` does a server-side and client-side check to determine when the client should stop polling for logs. IE: if UTC server write time - client's current time >= 30 min and there's no logs, stop polling. Sometimes clients' clocks aren't synchronized and this can cause the client to immediately stop polling for additional logs, which is an unfriendly user experience. The fix for this would be to make it purely server or client side based rather than mixing and matching them. Another approach would be to make it strictly logical rather than physical time.

issue_title: Support using existing Docker images as hooks
issue_body:
Currently a `language: docker` hook only supports running a Docker image which it built itself (via a `Dockerfile`).

However this seems a little unnecessary and expensive since we can and should be building and publishing our Docker images to a registry.

Would there be interest in supporting using existing images as hooks? It should be simple and I'd be happy to make the PR.

## Proposed `.pre-commit-hooks.yaml` change

```yaml
-   id: flake8-docker
    name: flake8 (via docker)
    description: flake8, but provided by docker
    files: \.py$

    language: docker
    # Image to `docker pull`
    docker_image: my.registry.example.com/pre-commit-hooks/flake8:latest
    # Optional: use `ENTRYPOINT` of the image if not specified
    entry: flake8
```

issue_title: Make resolving a url easier
issue_body:
## Long story short

Assuming there's a resource `/foo/bar/`, I want (like in django) a request to `/foo/bar` to be redirected (301) to `/foo/bar/`. 

I don't want this to be built into aiohttp, it's very simple in middleware and belongs outside the main repo, however currently it involves calling numerous private methods.

## Expected behaviour

`request.app.router.resolve_str('/foo/bar/')` (or whatever) to return information about the associated resource

## Actual behaviour

My current solution is

```python
except HTTPException as e:
    if request.method == METH_GET and e.status == 404 and not request.rel_url.raw_path.endswith('/'):
        possible_path = request.rel_url.raw_path + '/'
        for resource in request.app.router._resources:
            match_dict = resource._match(possible_path)
            if match_dict:
                raise HTTPMovedPermanently(possible_path)
```

Note the use of private properties.

```python
from collections import namedtuple

from sanic.exceptions import NotFound


def _row_namedtuple(cursor):
    return namedtuple('Row', _field_names(cursor))


def _field_names(cursor):
    return [name[0].lower() for name in cursor.description]


class DbExecutor:
    def __init__(self, sql, pool, params=()):
        self.sql = sql
        self.params = params
        self.pool = pool
        self._connection = None
        self._cursor = None

    async def __aenter__(self):
        self._connection = await self.pool.acquire()
        self._cursor = await self._connection.cursor()
        await self._cursor.execute(self.sql, self.params)

    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):
        await self._cursor.close()
        await self._connection.close()
```

issue_title: grad of anp.abs and anp.absolute returning NaN when input is zero
issue_body:
anp.abs and anp.absolute return NaN when one of the inputs is identically zero:
`>> grad(anp.abs)(0.0)`

Note that anp.fabs does not have this issue.

The reason is the division by ans in the definition of the abs grad:
anp.abs.defgrad(     lambda ans, x : lambda g : g * anp.conj(x) / ans)

https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/master/autograd/numpy/numpy_grads.py#L96


I'm not sure what is the best way to fix this, as naive solutions involve assigning to an array which would break when taking higher order derivatives.
