issue_title: Bibtex Strings question
issue_body:
Hello, I can't find much information about the implementation of bibtex strings on the readthedocs site. I have been parsing BibTex files generated by NASA ADS, for example [this Ph.D. Thesis](https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/#abs/2008PhDT.........1N/abstract). If you go to the left panel and choose export... in BibTex, you will notice that it includes the line
`month = January`, which crashes the BibTexParser even when `common_strings=True`. What is the recommended way to work around that? I tried appending an `@STRINGS` section to the top of the BibTex, but that didn't help (BibTexParser reported that it skipped a `strings` section for being non-standard). I eventually made my own set of string definition tuples and added them manually, but that doesn't seem like the best idea. 
```python
monthstrings = [('january','January'),...]
parser = bibtexparser.bparser.BibTexParser(common_strings=True)
parser.bib_database.strings.update(monthstrings)
```

My other question relates to how the library writes bibtex files. For me it will write either `month={January}` or `month={jan}` and I can't tell how it chooses one or the other. Is there a way for me to standardize everything to `month = jan`? I'm guessing that when there are brackets around the month it is being treated as a raw string instead of a bibtex string, so if the parser reads in `month={jan}` it will write out `month={jan}` but if it reads in `month = jan` it will write out `month={January}`.

Thanks in advance for your help!

issue_title: Need upgrade path from non-reactive charms to reactive

Part of this can be done in an `@hook('upgrade-charm')` handler, but we at a minimum need some sort of helper to get the interface layers to the correct state.

issue_title: db.__call__ return a result proxy?
issue_body:
It's becoming pretty common for queries to return large results sets that take a long time to return. We might want to change `db.__call__` to return a result proxy that needs to be fetched like `db(**query).fetch()`. In early days we were reluctant to do that in the original pass because we were getting pushback on the verbosity of the API, but ultimately I think that was the wrong call. Returning a result proxy would make things fast and also allow us to incorporate things like `limit` which are hard to fit into our elided query API.

I think we can leave `db.__getitem__` as it is because those query results sets scale with the size of the input argument. Since that's the API that people use the most, this might not even break very much user code. Of course it will break some.

issue_title: Need to do a better job documenting SDK and Runtime dependencies
issue_body:
See: https://github.com/dotnet/core/issues/447#issuecomment-307462749

This needs to happen here: https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/Documentation/prereqs.md

issue_title: Link for "Download the latest .NET Core SDK" on the main page is broken
issue_body:
# Issue Title

Link for "Download the latest .NET Core SDK" on the main page (URL https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/release-notes/download-archives/2.1.302-download.md) is broken

# General

Thanks to the dotnet core team for a brilliant product / ecosystem. One issue is that I often find versioning difficult i.e. it's hard to find a "single source of truth" that will tell me which is the latest release version, which is the latest prerelease version etc.

issue_title: 'libhostpolicy.so' required to execute the application was not found
issue_body:
I created an MVC .Net Core 1.1 application. It works on windows and I'd like to run in my raspberry py too.
But It doesn't work.

## Environment:
1. VS17 enterprise
2. Mvc .net core 1.1
3. Raspberry pi with Debian 9.4
4. Repo on github

## Expected behavior
After publishing in windows for linux-arm I have pull my repo into raspberry, but when 
I run 
`dotnet  myapp.dll`
I have the error:
_A fatal error was encountered. The library 'libhostpolicy.so' required to execute the application was not found in [path]_

## Actual behavior
It doesn't work and I don't know how to solve

Running `dotnet publish` on my windows 10 it generates DLLs and it runs without issues.
With the same codebase on a linux container (FROM microsoft/dotnet:2.0.0-sdk) if I run `dotnet publish` and try to start my application (`dotnet mydll.dll`) it returns the following error:

dotnet version linux: 2.0.0

issue_title: Client integration testing shouldn't use monolithic boulder binary
issue_body: Per https://github.com/letsencrypt/boulder/issues/482, the client integration test uses the boulder monolithic binary (start.sh). Instead it should use start.py, which runs the VA, SA, RA, CA, and WFE as separate processes and requires AMQP to talk between them. This is more consistent with the production environment and will help find bugs in the RPC code.

issue_title: Need Technical Details documentation
issue_body:
Notes, like the following, deserve to live somewhere:

Conx builds models between any two banks that have fully activated incoming pathways. 

Therefore, Network.propagate_from() won't work if you attempt to propagate through a merge (concat, add, mult, etc) layer. 

It also doesn't give you multiple pathways when they exist. For example, if you have two pathways between banks "input" and "hidden5", it will just give you the last one.

Conx could also be enhanced to allow propagations that involve multiple input/output banks. I think one can do that manually now, but requires you to hand-wire the model using connections between banks.

issue_title: Add LRU cache to gettz and factories
issue_body:
Now that #635 is resolved, `gettz`, `tzoffset` and `tzstr` satisfies the property that two `tzinfo` objects (`tz1` and `tz2`) constructed with identical arguments will always be the same object (`tz1` is `tz2`), but the cached objects expire whenever no instances of a given timezone exist outside of the cache. This satisfies the "timezone semantics" reasons for adding a cache and will have good performance characteristics so long as at least one object holds a strong reference to the time zone.

But this *does* sacrifice performance for people who would prefer a cache because they are creating the same object over and over again even though it goes out of scope every time, so for example:

```python
def NY_timestamp(dt):
    dt.astimezone(tz.gettz('America/New_York')
    return dt.timestamp()

a = []
for i in range(10000):
    a.append(NY_timestamp(datetime.now()))
```

I think the above would needlessly create 10000 copies of `tz.gettz('America/New_York')`, when even a modest LRU cache would save the trouble.

I think we should leave the `_instances` dictionary as a `WeakValueDictionary` and add a second `_lru_cache` dictionary that holds a limited (and configurable) number of strong references to used time zones. Not sure the best implementation of the cache itself, preferably something where left-append, right-pop and reordering a single element are all O(1), so I think something implemented with a doubly-linked list (maybe `OrderedDict`).

issue_title: tzrange offsets can be outside of -24:00/24:00 range
issue_body:
Noticed this when reviewing #581:

```python
from dateutil import tz
from datetime import *

tz1 = tz.tzstr('WGT30')
tz2 = tz.tzrange('WGT', timedelta(hours=-30))

dt1 = datetime(2011, 1, 1, tzinfo=tz1)
dt2 = datetime(2011, 1, 1, tzinfo=tz2)

for dt in [dt1, dt2]:
    try:
        dt.utcoffset()
    except ValueError as e:
        print(e)

# Result:
# offset must be a timedelta strictly between -timedelta(hours=24) and timedelta(hours=24).
# offset must be a timedelta strictly between -timedelta(hours=24) and timedelta(hours=24).
```

Simple matter of checking the range in the constructor. Not sure if `tzwin` is also affected.

issue_title: RFC822 timestamp using GMT is parsed into BST if local time is UK time
issue_body:
The following code snipped prints `True` unless the machine is using UK time.

```python
from datetime import datetime
from dateutil.parser import parse
from dateutil.tz import tzutc


d1 = datetime(2002, 10, 2, 13, 0, tzinfo=tzutc())
d2 = parse('Wed, 02 Oct 2002 13:00:00 GMT')
print(d1 == d2)
```

It appears that at [this point](https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/blob/master/dateutil/parser.py#L605) in the parse function, the parser sees that local time is one of `('GMT', 'BST')` because in the Summer the UK uses BST (GMT+1).

Source issue: boto/botocore#1070

issue_title: Windows Python seems to lack locale.LC_MESSAGES
issue_body:
When I attempt to open a file in a .git repository with Vim + Powerline I get the following error displayed in Vim's command line:

    AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'LC_MESSAGES'

If I execute the :messages command in Vim's commandline I get the following stack trace:

    "D:\Users\Wolf\Copy\Documents\Projects\Visual Studio\Visual Studio 2015\Projects\SteamDesktopAuthenticator\lib\SteamAuth\SteamAuth\TimeAligner.cs" [unix] 84L, 2776C
    2016-03-23 04:26:15,427:ERROR:vim:file_vcs_status:Exception while computing segment: 'module' object has no attribute 'LC_MESSAGES'
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Vim\vimfiles\bundle\powerline\powerline\segment.py", line 173, in process_segment
        contents = segment['contents_func'](pl, segment_info)
      File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Vim\vimfiles\bundle\powerline\powerline\segment.py", line 412, in <lambda>
        contents_func = lambda pl, segment_info: _contents_func(pl=pl, segment_info=segment_info, **args)
      File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Vim\vimfiles\bundle\powerline\powerline\segments\vim\__init__.py", line 525, in file_vcs_status
        status = repo.status(os.path.relpath(name, repo.directory))
      File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Vim\vimfiles\bundle\powerline\powerline\lib\vcs\git.py", line 82, in status
        extra_ignore_files=tuple(join(gitd, x) for x in ('logs/HEAD', 'info/exclude')),
      File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Vim\vimfiles\bundle\powerline\powerline\lib\vcs\__init__.py", line 131, in get_file_status
        file_status_cache[keypath] = ans = get_func(directory, file_path)
      File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Vim\vimfiles\bundle\powerline\powerline\lib\vcs\git.py", line 177, in do_status
        return next(self._gitcmd(directory, 'status', '--porcelain', '--ignored', '--', path))[:2]
      File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Vim\vimfiles\bundle\powerline\powerline\lib\shell.py", line 58, in readlines
        encoding = get_preferred_input_encoding()
      File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Vim\vimfiles\bundle\powerline\powerline\lib\encoding.py", line 61, in get_preferred_input_encoding
        locale.getlocale(locale.LC_MESSAGES)[1]

Upon examining the method get_preferred_input_encoding() in lib\encoding.py I found the issue to be the following line of code:

    locale.getlocale(locale.LC_MESSAGES)[1]

    Note: get_preferred_output_encoding() also contains this line.

So when I call this code directly in a python 2.7.10 instance, I got the same AttributeError: (happens in clean virtual machine images of Windows 7 and Windows 10 + fresh installs of Python 2.7.10 so its either a windows-only bug or it just happens for this version of Python):

    Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.10240]
    (c) 2015 Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.

    C:\Users\Wolf>python
    Python 2.7.10 (default, May 23 2015, 09:40:32) [MSC v.1500 32 bit (Intel)] on win32
    Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
    >>> import locale
    >>> locale.getlocale(locale.LC_MESSAGES)[1]
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
    AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'LC_MESSAGES'
    >>>

And in my own testing I was able to fix the issue for get_preferred_output_encoding(), and similarly for get_preferred_input_encoding(), with the following modification:

if hasattr(locale, 'LC_MESSAGES'):
	return (
		locale.getlocale(locale.LC_MESSAGES)[1]
		or locale.getdefaultlocale()[1]
		or 'ascii'
	)

issue_title: Some change in the Docs required , for security reasons json can't pass arrray
issue_body:
Docs to serialise SQLAlchemy with multiple rows suggest the code bellow:
```python
users_schema = UserSchema(many=True)

@app.route('/api/users/')
def users():
    all_users = User.all()
    result = users_schema.dump(all_users)
    return jsonify(result.data)
    # OR
    # return user_schema.jsonify(all_users)
```

This code will actually give error because list objects can't be jsonified, (ref this)[https://github.com/pallets/flask/issues/673]

Instead the example should be:
```python
users_schema = UserSchema(many=True)

@app.route('/api/users/')
def users():
    all_users = User.all()
    result = users_schema.dump(all_users)
    return jsonify(result.data)
    # OR
    # return user_schema.jsonify({'data':result.data})
```

issue_title: Retire env vars, System properties?
issue_body:
I am working on fixing calls to the deprecated `Engine.init` from tests. The only thing making it somewhat difficult is that configuration is passed around via system properties, not a config object (like the Spark config), which makes testing hard in the new code path.

More generally, there are some instances where BigDL takes input from environment vars, and some where it uses System properties, and some where it uses Spark config. I think it is generally bad practice to use env vars or System properties; just makes things difficult to test and encapsulate. At least, seems unnecessary to use env vars and properties separately.

Is any non-Spark usage actually supported? could config actually just use the Spark config object alone? that is can "local" mode go away?

It seems early enough in the project that changes like this are easy to make so wanted to raise it.

issue_title: Nested serializers only work with Django Model objects
issue_body:
While DRF works perfectly fine with simple non-model instances, DJA crashes and can't be made to work correctly.

For example, this code works fine with DRF:

```python
import random
from rest_framework.response import Response
from rest_framework.views import APIView
from rest_framework_json_api import serializers
from rest_framework_json_api.renderers import JSONRenderer as jsonapi_renderer


class Post(object):
    def __init__(self, message):
        super(Post, self).__init__()
        self.pk = 'tmp-%s' % random.randint(1, 2**20)  # Added to support json API referencing
        self.message = message


class User(object):
    def __init__(self, name, posts):
        super(User, self).__init__()
        self.pk = 'tmp-%s' % random.randint(1, 2**20)  # Added to support json API referencing
        self.name = name
        self.posts = posts


class PostSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    message = serializers.CharField(max_length=200)


class UserSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    name = serializers.CharField(max_length=200)
    posts = PostSerializer(many=True, read_only=True)


class UserView(APIView):
    resource_name = 'users'
    renderer_classes = (jsonapi_renderer,)  # ! Uncommenting this causes failure !

    def get(self, request, format=None):
        p1 = Post("First message")
        p2 = Post("Second message")
        user = User('Bob', [p1, p2])
        s = UserSerializer(user)
        return Response(s.data)
```
However, after uncommenting the line that specifies the JSON API renderer class to be used, the view crashes with:

`'NoneType' object has no attribute 'Meta'`

If DJA does not support Model-less references, it could at least be clearly stated in the documentation, mentioning also some workarounds if such exist. Though, I have not found any way to make this working yet.

issue_title: print_filtered_stacktrace hides chained exceptions
issue_body:
```python
import sacred

try:
    raise Exception('e1') from Exception('e2')
except Exception as e:
    print('#### Message from print_filtered_stacktrace ####')
    sacred.utils.print_filtered_stacktrace()
    print('#### Original message ####')
    raise e
```
```
#### Message from print_filtered_stacktrace ####
Traceback (most recent calls WITHOUT Sacred internals):
  File "a.py", line 3, in <module>
    raise Exception('e1') from Exception('e2')
Exception: e1
#### Original message ####
Exception: e2

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "a.py", line 8, in <module>
    raise e
  File "a.py", line 3, in <module>
    raise Exception('e1') from Exception('e2')
Exception: e1
```
print_filtered_stacktrace is used at https://github.com/IDSIA/sacred/blob/master/sacred/experiment.py#L255, and it hides the traceback from chained exceptions.

print_filtered_stacktrace doesn't call  traceback.print_exception but only use traceback.format_exception_only in case that "WITHOUT Sacred internals", and it never show chained exceptions.

issue_title: vc with rlimit taking too much time
issue_body:
Hello,

I have spent some time looking at the rlimit option. Overall it seems to work very well. On the vast majority of our VCs, we found that z3 consumes hundreds of thousands of rlimit units (steps) in less than a second.

But on the file [rlimit.smt2](https://gist.github.com/kanigsson/5e579a7a2701f6a8aa41ca400ce5f626), z3 consumes much less steps, so that even after running for 30 seconds, it only has only consumed 100000 steps. As a consequence, z3 will run for a very long time on this VC with an rlimit that only lasts a second on other VCs.

 $ z3 -st -T:30 rlimit.smt2
timeout
(:max-memory   14.37
 :memory       11.41
 :num-allocs   192107538686.00
 :rlimit-count 102154
 :time         29.99
 :total-time   29.99)

So I suspect that the rlimit is not counted in some part of Z3 that consumes a lot of CPU time in this VC.

Note that we don't really care about the exact count of rlimit steps, or it's equivalence in time.

Thanks in advance

Johannes

issue_title: memory corruption when execution is interrupted by memory_max_alloc
issue_body:
Hello Z3 team,

I noticed we sometimes get an error message:
      *** Error in `z3': double free or corruption (!prev): 0x0b2ef5e8 ***
      Abandon (core dumped)
when Z3 is interrupted by a memory_max_alloc constraint.

For example, it is the case on my laptop (linux 32bits) and the unstable version of Z3 4.4.1 on the example below with the command:
     z3 memory_max_alloc_count=200000 my_file.smt2

Do you think that you can easily do something about it or that we should rather work-around this problem on our side by ignoring the error message ?

Thanks in advance,
Claire

issue_body:
For this input:

```
(set-logic ALL)
(declare-fun s0 () String)
(declare-fun s1 () String)
(declare-fun s2 () Int)
(define-fun s4 () Bool (>= s2 0))

issue_title: Z3 opt fails when minimizing BV on unsat instances
issue_body:
The following example
```
from z3 import *

x = BitVec("x", 64)

zero = BitVecVal(0, 64)
one = BitVecVal(1, 64)

s = Optimize()
s.add(one < zero, x > 0)

# h = s.maximize(-x)
h = s.minimize(x)

res = s.check()

print(h.lower())
print(h.upper())
print(res)
print(s.model())
```
fails with
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test.py", line 14, in <module>
    res = s.check()
  File "src/api/python/z3.py", line 6519, in check
    return CheckSatResult(Z3_optimize_check(self.ctx.ref(), self.optimize))
  File "src/api/python/z3core.py", line 3523, in Z3_optimize_check
    err = lib().Z3_get_error_code(a0)
z3types.Z3Exception: Sort mismatch at argument #1 for function (declare-fun - (Int) Int) supplied sort is (_ BitVec 64)
```

If `s.minimize(x)` is changed to `s.maximize(-x)`, everything works as expected.

issue_title: Relu weight init doc problem?
issue_body:
From the [doc](http://lasagne.readthedocs.org/en/latest/modules/init.html#lasagne.init.Glorot) or [source](https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/init.py#L103), it says:

```{python}
For a :class:`DenseLayer <lasagne.layers.DenseLayer>`, if ``gain='relu'``
    and ``initializer=Uniform``, the weights are initialized as
    .. math::
       a &= \\sqrt{\\frac{6}{fan_{in}+fan_{out}}}\\\\
       W &\sim U[-a, a]
```
But from what I read from the souce, the formula is true for a sigmoid activator. In case of Relu, it should be `U[-sqrt(2)a, sqrt(2)a]`, as the gain is `sqrt(2)`.

Am I right?

issue_title: receptive_field_size in the implementation of the Glorot initialization
issue_body:
I am confused about the formula in the Glorot initialization:
`std = self.gain * np.sqrt(2.0 / ((n1 + n2) * receptive_field_size))`
in https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/init.py#L103-L177

Assume we have a W with shape (num_filters, num_input_channels, filter_length_1, filter_length_2)
When we do convolution, we are doing a weighted sum with a patch with shape (num_input_channels,  filter_length_1, filter_length_2) and we get an output with shape (num_filters). 

If this is correct, isn't it that the `input size=num_input_channels*filter_length_1*filter_length_2`, the `output size=num_filters`, and the formula is `std = self.gain * np.sqrt(2.0 / (n1 + n2 * receptive_field_size))`?

Then, why there is a receptive_field_size attached to n1, too?

issue_title: [Not an issue] Proposal for metric accumulation
issue_body:
I've written minor class, which could be useful to lasagne users when averaging loss values / accuracies / whatever over minibatches.

Since in most cases I'm only `.append`ing something to list, I found this useful to append to lists during tuple unpacking.

I've published some [demo](http://arogozhnikov.github.io/2016/05/22/new-item-for-lists.html) how Lasagne-based code could benefit from such feature.

It would be nice to hear your suggestions or criticism.

issue_title: Issue with LSTM output
issue_body:
I noticed LSTM layer output is a little bit off. 

Even with sigmoid activation I would sometimes get 1 < output or output < 0. 

The value of the cell to output gate was being added after the activation function when peepholes are enabled.

At line 970~ in recurrent.py
```python
            outgate = self.nonlinearity_outgate(outgate)

            # Compute new cell value
            cell = forgetgate*cell_previous + ingate*cell_input

            if self.peepholes:
                outgate += cell*self.W_cell_to_outgate
```

should be changed to:
```python
            # Compute new cell value
            cell = forgetgate*cell_previous + ingate*cell_input

            if self.peepholes:
                outgate += cell*self.W_cell_to_outgate
	
            outgate = self.nonlinearity_outgate(outgate)
```

I'm pretty sure there's a way to pull or push or something but I don't know how. No real experience with Github. I hope I helped anyway.

issue_title: roi pixel mask shape
issue_body: The `pixel_mask` argument of `PlaneSegmentation.add_roi` is currently allowed to be (n, (x,y,v)) where n is the number of pixels, x and y are the coordinates of the pixel, and v is the value. If you try to make an roi that is 3D: (n, (x,y,z,v)), there will be an error during the `io.write` call. I would prefer if we allowed (n, (x,y,z,v)), since rois are sometimes defined in 3D and I think it will be tricky to define 3D rois in any other way. If for some reason we need to keep this only in a single plane, then I would at least like to catch this error on the `add_roi` call, not during `io.write`

issue_title: `ModelForm` with a `MoneyField` generates deprecation warning
issue_body:
This code uses `forms.MoneyField.__init__(currency_choices=...)`:
https://github.com/django-money/django-money/blob/master/djmoney/models/fields.py#L412

But this code then deprecates that:
https://github.com/django-money/django-money/blob/master/djmoney/forms/fields.py#L35

resulting in a `PendingDeprecationWarning` that can't be fixed by users of the library.

One of them should be fixed, presumably the former.

Thanks!

The [RFP for Second Round of Mozilla Science Mini-Grants is now open](https://science.mozilla.org/blog/2018-mini-grant-rfp) and folks should apply!

These are the main categories they're interested in:

> * Prototyping – Building tools or documentation collaboratively with others. This can be open source code, documentation, tools and technologies used in data-driven science, scientific software, discovery tools (e.g., ways of searching code, hosting services, etc.)
> * Community Building – These might include mentorship programs, workshops, meet-ups or other events that promote community engagement.
> * Curriculum – Materials might include text, visuals, and video for online or in-person training.

Within each of those, I've got some ideas. I'll leave a separate comment though so it's just part of the main thread rather than in the issue body.

Deadline is _this Sunday_, December 17, 2017.

issue_title: Launch stability issues
issue_body:
As mentioned in https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/7037, we've got ourselves a persnickety problem on OS X (whose solution can affect other platforms).

We're running into an issue where command line parameter behavior is different when packaged vs. run in dev mode. When running locally in dev mode, arguments come the standard argv way which means we handle them in the ready event.

When packaged, arguments end up going to the open-file event (cool!), yet we still end up with the ready event being fired and processing the argv. Our default mode is to open a fresh new file. At this point, we don't know if the open-file event is going to happen later as a result of running it from the command line.

issue_title: In-door flight with RC_overrides
issue_body:
I have posted the following question in another issue, by I think it's better to raise a new one.
 
I have been using Dronekit-Python to do RC_overrides for a while, I am now able to arm, take off, hover, and land in a GPS-denied environment. 

I am currently working with Pixhawk v2, and a Raspberry Pi 3B, but I will probably move onto APM2.6 for some other reasons. (the connection is fine, but I'm not sure whether APM2.6 can give the same responses)

My questions are:

1. By lots of experiments, I found out that the minimum time interval between each overrides is 0.393 s. Once set shorter, some values will be ignored. But my project concerns some real-time controls, so is it possible to cut down the time intervals? 
(for example: In the example 'basic mission', we can download, clear, create and upload a mission from/to the flight controller, is it possible to upload a RC_overrides mission onto the flight controller, so the the overrides can be done on board the flight controller? )

2. Since setting RC_channel values is not equal to setting the attitudes (yaw/pitch/roll), a specific override, for example, with channel 2, will cause the UAV to go forward or backward(depending on your value) at a different speed. Can you provide an example concerning the control of movements via setting attitudes(yaw/pitch/roll)?

3. I understand that in a GPS-denied environment, I can not arm in guided mode. Hence, I can not take off using simple_take_off or do any velocity controls. But I am up to modify and compile the source of AC 3.2.1, can you give me some hints to start with. I want to take off using simple_take_off and hover at a desired altitude. My quad has a sonar (MB1240 to be specific), so it should have the necessary feedback to do the trick.

Thank you.

issue_title: Potential performance fix
issue_body:
I'm trying to do some dev work/testing on AFS and handling large objects is extremely expensive.

In paticular I'm talking about XML files we generate which are ~90Mb or so in size.

I suspect that a lot of the bottleneck in automatic flushes come from the pause of waiting for the file transactions to complete.

Would it make sense to introduce some locking queue of 1 thread which allows us to queue writing the XML string to disk and then release objects to carry on doing whatever the user/monitoring system wants to do with it?

Locking large jobs for a very long transaction like this on AFS is slightly annoying from an end-user perspective.

issue_title: Ganga DIRAC integration/communications
issue_body:
OK, @po10 met with some of the LHCbDirac developers today at CERN and there have been a few questions asked which I'd like to get people's opinions on with regard to communication between Ganga and Dirac.

### DIRAC and Ganga in the same Python environment?

As far as I know Ganga and DIRAC run in different Python environments due to the 2 projects having incompatible environments in the past.
Would we still like to continue down this route or is there any feeling that we can have Ganga work within the same environment as DIRAC?
(We have reduced the number of non pip-installable externals and we've recently improved our support for different IPython versions etc so would it be possible to test/use a version of Python/IPython which ships with a DIRAC install?)

If we chose to keep Ganga and DIRAC environments separate it may be nice to move back to a server/client model which would allow us to change the ```evaluate``` method to simply be something like ```evaluateOnServer``` which reduces the impact of the change on our current code to the 1 function as everywhere else would still work the same.
Does anyone have any good suggestions on how this may be implemented in a good way? @alexanderrichards  I know you mentioned you've looked into this a little and have some ideas.

### Should we group commonly grouped DIRAC calls into a single command which we execute within a DIRAC namespace?

It may be beneficial to simply rewrite some more complex functions such that we have a single ```finalizeDIRACJob``` method and equivalent for submitting subjobs to the DIRAC backend which we call once.
(there are technical reasons why submitting jobs this way may be tricky but the finalization of a DIRAC hello world job can probably be improved on compared to what we're doing now)

These methods would return a larger dictionary back to user within Ganga but runs a bit more of the risk of timeouts.

### Where are we best investing the dev time?

#### Option a) move back to a server/client model:
A server/client model sounds like a very nice long term solution but has a lot of potential complexity involved I suspect.

#### Option b) group DIRAC commands which can all be reasonably sent at the same time.

Grouping DIRAC calls from within Ganga to reduce the amount of overhead is potentially a nice solution (and possibly quicker to implement) but it doesn't get rid of all of our overheads it simply reduces them by a large factor.



(NB: I'm not talking about bulk submission here which would reduce the overhead of the submission of many jobs. I'm also not talking about #370 which I believe is a Ganga issue which can be solved well in 6.1.20.)




My vote would for trying **Option b)** and seeing what improvements this brings, what does everyone think?

issue_title: exception thrown on global rs::context construction using statically linked libs
issue_body:
An error occurs in a simple 10 line program when using statically linked librealsense, on windows, and seems to happen when rs context is constructed in a global outside of main.

Using statically linked versions of the realsense libraries, an error occurs in the following short program:

```c++
#define NOMINMAX
#include "../../include/librealsense/rs.hpp"
#include "../../include/librealsense/rsutil.h"

#ifdef _WIN64
#ifdef _DEBUG
#pragma comment(lib, "../../librealsense.vc14/realsense-s/obj/Debug-x64/realsense-sd.lib") 
#else  // release x64
#pragma comment(lib, "..../../librealsense.vc14/realsense-s/obj/Release-x64/realsense-s.lib")   
#endif // _DEBUG vs release for x64
#else  // we have _WIN32
#ifdef _DEBUG
#pragma comment(lib, "../../librealsense.vc14/realsense-s/obj/Debug-Win32/realsense-sd.lib")  
#else  // release 32 
#pragma comment(lib, "../../librealsense.vc14/realsense-s/obj/Release-Win32/realsense-s.lib")   // pragma easier than using settings within project properties
#endif // _DEBUG vs release for Win32 
#endif // _WIN64 vs _WIN32

rs::context ctx;  // in global scope this causes 'access violation' and throws exception on program startup
int main(int, const char**)
{
	return 0;
}
```
But putting the context on the stack, constructing after main is invoked, then the bug does not happen:
```c++
int main(int, const char**)
{
	rs::context ctx;  // fine when on the stack here 
	return 0;
}
```
So this indicates something isn't handled properly during the construction of the rs::context within the global scope of a program.   

The cause of the error appears to happen during a mutex/lock operation:
```c++
    Exception thrown at 0x536039E3 (msvcp140d.dll) in unit_test_g_ctx_construction.exe: 0xC0000005: Access violation reading location 0x00000000.
```

The call stack at runtime:
```c++
          testrs.exe!rs_context::acquire_instance() Line 38	C++
          testrs.exe!rs_create_context(int api_version, rs_error * * error) Line 48	C++
          testrs.exe!rs::context::context() Line 173	C++
          testrs.exe!`dynamic initializer for 'ctx''() Line 21	C++
```          

The project file used for this test was a newly created project file from vs2015 with all default settings.  hence the include pathing and library pragmas at the beginning of the source code shown above.  
I can push this unit test into a github branch if you are not able to reproduce.

| Firmware Version | all  |

issue_title: String References not working with `InputFields`
issue_body:
In order to overcome some circular dependencies I switched over my Schema to use String References as mentioned in this issue: https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene/issues/110

However, I've run into some issues with my `InputObjectType` classes.  So looking at something of the form:

```
# category_type.py
@schema.register
class CategoryInput(graphene.InputObjectType):
    Post = graphene.List('PostInput')

# post_type.py
@schema.register
class PostInput(graphene.InputObjectType):
    category = graphene.InputField('CategoryInput')
```

The `InputField` referencing 'CategoryInput' triggers an [AssertionError](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core/blob/master/graphql/type/definition.py#L618) (`AssertionError: PostInput.category field type must be Input Type but got: CategoryInput.`) saying that it's expecting an InputType as opposed to 'CategoryInput'.  However, the `List` seems to work correctly.  Taking a cursory glance at the code I'm thinking it's not correctly converting the String reference to the `InputObjectType`.

Any ideas?

issue_title: Add Documentation for Relay Edge implementation
issue_body:
In the [Relay mutation docs](https://facebook.github.io/relay/docs/guides-mutations.html#range-add), performing **RANGE_ADD** requires that the new edge created by the mutation is provided in the payload.

I do not know how to provide the correct cursor/`Edge` construction within the `relay.ClientIDMutation` subclass. 

The following code I wrote:

`new_object_edge = graphene.Field('ObjectEdge')`

 I believed would solve my issue, with a proper `resolve_new_object_edge(...)` resolver.

However, my resolver cannot reference the `relay.ConnectionField` that is specified on one of the other object types in my mutation, since it only has the context of my (in this case, Django) objects.

I experimented with `relay.Edge.for_node(SomeObjectType)` as well but all of the solutions I have tried so far modify the schema correctly but cannot return the appropriate `Edge`.

The implementation of this scenario in javascript can be found [here](https://github.com/facebook/relay/blob/master/examples/todo/data/schema.js#L140) in the Relay examples directory.

Any idea of a best approach? I can take this question to stack overflow, but felt that people using Relay & Graphene would enjoy seeing an example solution in the docs.

issue_title: Django filter on related objects no longer works
SerializerMutation nested serializer with many=True expects a String
Bug with `only_fields` on SerializerMutation
issue_body:
I was creating a custom Mutation using the `SerializerMutation` base class and I tried to exclude all the fields using `only_fields` but the check is possibly wrong, here's the code I've used

```python
class CreateCompany(SerializerMutation):
    company = graphene.Field(CompanyType)
    errors = graphene.List(ErrorType)

    class Meta:
        serializer_class = CompanySerializer
        only_fields = ()
```

And this is the check that's failing: https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/rest_framework/mutation.py#L24

I think we should check if only_fields is not None, instead of checking if it is truthy 

I'm happy to submit a PR once we agree on an approach to fix this.

class Foo(Base):

issue_title: Output watchmaker version at debug loglevel
Potential security issues with `get_ip()` changes in v4
issue_body:
I'm a little concerned by the change to use `ipware.ip.get_ip()` in v4.

1. There is no documentation to suggest that `django-ipware` may need to be configured.
2. There is now no documentation on the potential attack vector if the proxy doesn't set `X-Forwarded-For` (or whatever the first header used in `IPWARE_META_PRECEDENCE_ORDER` is) explicitly, c.f. [`USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST`](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/ref/settings/#std:setting-USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST)
3. ~~`django-ipware` says to use `get_ip()` if the server is **not** accessible on the Internet, see [here](https://github.com/un33k/django-ipware#how-to-use).~~ (Although looking at the code the distinction between `get_ip()` and `get_real_ip()`, the latter doesn't count private IP addresses as "real". What they really mean here is use `get_ip()` if there are also clients that may connect from an internal private network otherwise use `get_real_ip()`.)
4. Previously `django-axes` allowed configuring the maximum number of proxies in case of spoofed entries. This is no longer possible. `django-ipware` provides `get_trusted_ip()` but it isn't particularly useful in it's current form.

It would probably be wise to recommend setting `IPWARE_META_PRECEDENCE_ORDER` to `['HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR', 'REMOTE_ADDR']` or at least indicate that this was the original behaviour. Checking all of these headers by default feels risky.

Forgive me, but this feels rushed and ill thought through. It was working fine for me and issue #275 and pull request #280 fail to even give any evidence of a problem. Every comment in #275 is regarding `AttributeError` when looking up `META` on `NoneType` - `django-ipware` is still using `request.META`. This feels like a push to remove code in favour of a dependency for minor gain.

issue_title: Allow to reload rendered panel separately
issue_body:
In a multiprocess environment (e.g. a staging system being a load balancer) you will run into "Data for this panel isn't available anymore. Please reload the page and retry." (#750).

However, reloading just the rendered panel (e.g. `/__debug__/render_panel/?store_id=X&panel_id=SQLPanel`) will eventually work.

There was some discussion about using different stores (#819), which might not be possible in the end.

However, a workaround for now would be allowing to reload a panel's content alone, without reloading the whole page.

(While the toolbar's data might not be pickleable, there could still be information stored using Django's cache to know if there should be information for a given `store_id`.)

issue_title: Do not throw exception when template is missing
issue_body:
Currently you might end up not being able to access the admin etc in case the template is missing.

Code: https://github.com/jazzband/django-debug-toolbar/blob/f73b69e70800195576cb1a85512bc5197c659d85/debug_toolbar/toolbar.py#L56-L72.

I think it might be better to log the exception (`logger.exception`), and then just render nothing instead.

The case at hand is that the DDT is being enabled based on a user's group, and when the app was missing in INSTALLED_APPS (due to wrong settings), it caused the template to not being collected with `collectstatic`.
With the exception being thrown, it would not be possible to remove the user from the group again (without using the shell or fixing the settings in the code).

issue_title: Support multiple file writers writing at once.
issue_body:
## Background

TensorBoard assigns one `DirectoryWatcher` per run. The `DirectoryWatcher` watches for new events being written to the directory and loads it into TensorBoard's multiplexer. Today, [the `DirectoryWatcher` iterates through events files (for a run) in lexicographic order](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard/blob/bf1e23229b8f9efbc5348ffbd7d9418c5fcb8b3c/tensorboard/backend/event_processing/directory_watcher.py#L29). After it has finished reading from an events file, it never reads from it again (and moves on to a subsequent events file).

This behavior means that TensorBoard does not support multiple file writers writing to the same run directory: TensorBoard would move on to a different events file B once it finishes reading events file A and would never go back to reading A despite how A is updated.

In turn, that problem blocks TensorFlow+TensorBoard's transition towards migrating `Estimator`, tflearn hooks, and other high-level TensorFlow constructs to using the `tf.contrib.summary`-style summaries. `tf.contrib.summary`-style summaries are written via a type of `SummaryWriter` different from the one that writes traditional-style (`tf.summary.*`) summaries, so users that intermingle the summaries will necessarily have to concurrently use 2 summary writers. Many users thoughout google3 intermingle the summaries, and the transition must happen soon.

issue_title: Thread device keeps registering newer dataset to Leader during dataset transition
issue_body:
Observe the phenomenon, for example, there are two nodes, one is Leader and another is Router. Leader and Router forms a partition respectively (Leader has a larger partition id than Router's). Configure an older active timestamp on Leader's partition (e.g. 10) and a newer active timestamp (e.g. 20) on Router's.
Then remove the whitelist and let Router can hear Leader's advertisement. Router will migrate to Leader's partition. And when receiving the Child Id Response, it will register to Leader with its newer active dataset. However, there are also Tlvs that would affect network connectivity included in this MGMT_ACTIVE_SET.req, so Leader moves the entire Active dataset to Pending dataset and starts the delay timer. Due to the new active timestamp does not take effect immediately on Leader's side. Router does not get the chance to update its `mNetwork` with `mLocal` version, it will keep registering the its own dataset to Leader until the delay timer expires.

issue_title: csvstat --names ignores the --skip-lines option
issue_body:
csvstat --names ignores the --skip-lines option. It always returns the names from the first row. The regular csvstat features do respect --skip-lines.

For example:

> $ cat a.csv
> a,b,c
> d,e,f,g
> 1,2,3,4
> $ csvstat --names a.csv
>   1: a
>   2: b
>   3: c
> $ csvstat --skip-lines 1 --names a.csv
>   1: a
>   2: b
>   3: c

(Thanks for the great set of csv tools.)

issue_title: certbot standalone renewal with a VPN tunnel
issue_body:
I was trying to renew a cert for one of my servers:

`./certbot certonly --standalone -d <my-domain-here>`

..and I kept seeing a failure that looked like this:

```
certbot urn:acme:error:connection :: The server could not connect to the client to verify the domain :: Timeout

To fix these errors, please make sure that your domain name was entered correctly and the DNSA/
AAAA record(s) for that domain contain(s) the right IP address.
Additionally, please check that your computer has a publicly routable IP address and that no firewalls are preventing the server from communicating with the client.
If you're using the webroot plugin, you should also verify that you are serving files from the webroot path you provided.
```

At first I found this error a bit odd but then it occurred to me that I had a VPN up on the box where I was issuing the `./certbot` renewal request and I didn't when I originally created the cert some time ago. At this point the error made sense (it being related to my IP address not being recognized by the remote challenge response server), I dropped the VPN, re-attempted the renewal and it worked without an issue.

My question is: is it possible to make renewal work without having to drop the VPN tunnel?

Thanks!

If I do not use `--quiet` option, the output is fairly verbose:

```
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Processing /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/blaah.com.conf
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Cert not yet due for renewal

The following certs are not due for renewal yet:
  /etc/letsencrypt/live/blaah.com/fullchain.pem (skipped)
No renewals were attempted.
No hooks were run.
```

## Desired behaviour

I'm running `certbot-auto renew` inside a cron job. And I would like to see output from `certbot-auto` in case of either an error or in case certbot actually renewed some certificate. This would make it easier to troubleshoot the cron job. Also, as the actual renewal only happens once per month, it is not too much output. At least for me.

So, I would like a `--print-renewals` flag (used in combination with `--quiet` flag) that would allow be to tell `certbot-auto` to print out information about attempted renewals and their result (success or failure). And, in case no renewal is needed, certbot would not output anything (as is usual with `--quiet` flag).

issue_title: %chart does not support queries written in Standard SQL
issue_body:
%chart magic fails when queries use Standard SQL, regardless of whether the module referenced specifies dialect.

To validate:
When "myModule" is defined as:
%%sql 
SELECT a, b FROM [table]

The following works:
%chart bars -d myModule -f a,b 

However, if "myModule" is defined as:
%%sql
SELECT a,b FROM `table`

The same command (%chart bars -d myModule -f a,b) fails.

Either adding a dialect flag for chart or respecting flags from the module would suffice to solve this.

issue_title: Convert polylines and polygons into paths
issue_body:
Polylines and polygons are defined in the SVG specification as being alternate forms for paths starting with an absolute move to the first point and linetos to each of the following points. In the case of polygon, a closepath is also added.

A naive reading of this feature request would just convert "polyline points=..." into "path d=M..." and "polygon points=..." into "path d=M...Z", but the real savings is had from:

* vertical and horizontal line segments;
* short line segments enabling shorter relative linetos.

Consider this polygon, from a test case in https://bugs.launchpad.net/scour/+bug/481605/comments/3 :

<polygon fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" fill="#993300" points="370.441,402.976 371.967,403.015 374.941,403.343 

	377.819,403.942 380.601,404.831 383.247,405.989 385.758,407.419 388.113,409.042 390.335,410.916 392.344,413.02 394.141,415.26 ..." />

A naive conversion would just do this:

<path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" fill="#993300" d="M370.441 402.976 371.967 403.015 374.941 403.343 377.819 403.942 380.601 404.831 383.247 405.989 385.758 407.419 388.113 409.042 390.335 410.916 392.344 413.02 394.141 415.26 ... Z" />

A real conversion would be done before paths, and thus allow the new path to be converted as well:

<path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" fill="#993300" d="m370.441 402.976 1.526.039 2.974.328 2.878.599 2.782.889 2.646 1.158 2.511 1.43 2.355 1.623 2.222 1.874 2.009 2.104 1.797 2.24 ... Z" />

which is clearly shorter.

issue_title: Can't prefetch base instance
issue_body:
Hi,

I am struggling to understand how to make my queries more efficient when the parent object must be accessed (use case that led me here is described below).

Here is a basic scenario:

    from django.db import models
    from polymorphic.models import PolymorphicModel

    class Base(PolymorphicModel):
        field1 = models.CharField(max_length=8)

    class Child1(Base):
        field2 = models.CharField(max_length=8)

All I want to do is to load both Child1 _and_ the base instance so that I can access both Child1 & its Base counterpart.

I create my object with:

    child1 = Child1(field1="a", field2="b")

And I fetch it with:
    
   child1 = Child1.objects.filter(pk=1)[0]

This triggers the following SQL query:

     SELECT "test_polymorphic_base"."id", "test_polymorphic_base"."polymorphic_ctype_id", "test_polymorphic_base"."field1", "test_polymorphic_child1"."base_ptr_id", "test_polymorphic_child1"."field2" FROM "test_polymorphic_child1" INNER JOIN "test_polymorphic_base" ON ("test_polymorphic_child1"."base_ptr_id" = "test_polymorphic_base"."id") WHERE "test_polymorphic_child1"."base_ptr_id" = 1  LIMIT 1; args=(1,)

Note that both field1 and field2 were successfully SELECTed, as well as any field allowing to directly instanciate the Base instance.
Calling `child1.field1` and `child1.field2` do not trigger additional query, as expected.

However, calling `child1.base_ptr` does:

    SELECT "test_polymorphic_base"."id", "test_polymorphic_base"."polymorphic_ctype_id", "test_polymorphic_base"."field1" FROM "test_polymorphic_base" WHERE "test_polymorphic_base"."id" = 1; args=(1,)

Note that all those fields were _already_ fetched with previous query. So I figure I'd use `.select_related`, but the result is the same:

    child1 = Child1.objects.select_related('base_ptr').filter(pk=1)[0] # child1.base_ptr triggers an extra query

I also tried `.prefetch_related`:

    child1 = Child1.objects.prefetch_related('base_ptr').filter(pk=1)[0]

Which fails immediately with:

    ValueError: 'base_ptr' does not resolve to an item that supports prefetching - this is an invalid parameter to prefetch_related().

This seems to be related to the fact that `Child1.base_ptr` does not have a `.get_prefetch_queryset()` method.

Now, my initial (and real) use case: when trying to delete children objects, the Django deletion collector performs (in this case) [`getattr(obj, 'base_ptr')`](https://github.com/django/django/blob/stable/2.1.x/django/db/models/deletion.py#L201), which triggers as described one spurious query for each item, even though all data required for deletion was already loaded in memory. This is greatly slowing down the delete process and seems unneeded:

    Child1.objects.select_related('base_ptr').delete()

    # Objects _and_ base fields are fetched when the queryset is loaded
    DEBUG:django.db.backends:(0.001) SELECT "test_polymorphic_base"."id", "test_polymorphic_base"."polymorphic_ctype_id", "test_polymorphic_base"."field1", "test_polymorphic_child1"."base_ptr_id", "test_polymorphic_child1"."field2" FROM "test_polymorphic_child1" INNER JOIN "test_polymorphic_base" ON ("test_polymorphic_child1"."base_ptr_id" = "test_polymorphic_base"."id"); args=()
    # Now we are in the deletion Collector .collect at line 201: useless query! There is one for each queryset object.
    DEBUG:django.db.backends:(0.000) SELECT "test_polymorphic_base"."id", "test_polymorphic_base"."polymorphic_ctype_id", "test_polymorphic_base"."field1" FROM "test_polymorphic_base" WHERE "test_polymorphic_base"."id" = 1; args=(1,)
    # Actual deletion
    DEBUG:django.db.backends:(0.000) DELETE FROM "test_polymorphic_child1" WHERE "test_polymorphic_child1"."base_ptr_id" IN (1); args=(1,)
    DEBUG:django.db.backends:(0.000) DELETE FROM "test_polymorphic_base" WHERE "test_polymorphic_base"."id" IN (1); args=(1,)

I figure that to prevent this, I'd need `child1.base_ptr` _not_ to trigger an extra query but I can't find a way to let Django know it already has everything it needs to instantiate it.

Am I missing something? Is there any way around this?

Thanks

issue_title: Active markdown cells.
issue_body:
Currently markdown cells are handled at the frontend and does not interact with the kernel, so it does not support string interpolation. It would be nice to allow sos to handle markdown cells so that we can handle string interpolation. There are two solutions:

1. Hijack notebook markdown cells. We cannot hijack keyboard events since the notebook can be rendered in batch, so we have to change the way markdown cells are handled. This would require us to change the prototype of [`TextCell`](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/notebook/static/notebook/js/textcell.js), which is to my understanding very difficult.

2. Create a new "kernel" type named "md". Basically the regular execute mechanism will be used to send the text to the sos kernel. SoS would do something to it (string interpolation) and send the text back through the msg channel. The frontend accepts the text and render it to HTML in place of the cell. The latter part can be a bit difficult (hide input cell etc) but should be doable by following the TextCell way.

This can be useful but I am not sure how to translate this to batch mode sos script.

issue_title: Actor placement and task scheduling are not aware of each other.
issue_body:
This is most relevant in the case of actors and tasks that both require GPUs (perhaps not the most common case).

Suppose that we have two machines each with 1 GPU. We create one actor that uses 1 GPU and one task that uses 1 GPU. It is possible that they will be placed on the same machine. In that case, if the actor is created first, the task will never get scheduled because the actor may be around forever.

```python
import ray
import time

# This example should be run on a two node cluster in which each node has a single GPU.
ray.init(redis_address=...)

@ray.actor(num_gpus=1)
class Foo(object):
  def __init__(self):
    pass

@ray.remote(num_gpus=1)
def f():
  return 1

# Create an actor. This uses 1 GPU.
Foo()

time.sleep(1)

# Wait for the GPU task to finish. This may hang.
ray.get(f.remote())
```

issue_title: clarify which Streamlink Plugins are supported
issue_body:
## Issue

- [x] This is not a bug report, feature request, or plugin (improvement) request.
- [x] I have read the contribution guidelines. <!-- Replace the space with an x to check the box: [x] -->

### Description

Ref https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/pull/1972#issuecomment-410530312

Having Plugins in the **master branch** which won't receive any updated when they break
does not make that much sense.

**They should all be removed,** if they are not supported for any future updates.

---

Here is a list of some plugins that might not match this

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#plugin-requests

and could be removed.

---

**This issue is there to decide**
- if they should be removed
- which should be removed
- which can stay (for example some of the broken plugins at the end)

---

> Sites which are hosting stolen content as their main source of content

- Aliez
- ArconaiTv
- SeeTV
- StreamBoat
- Weeb

---

> NSFW sites of a pornographic nature (cam sites, porn sites, etc.)
- bongacams
- Cam4
- Camsoda
- Chaturbate

---

> Sites which are mostly hosting VODs (stolen or legitimate)

- Antenna (broken old plugin)
- Dplay (broken old plugin, youtube-dl could be for this if someone wants this)

issue_title: [versioning] use minor version and free patch version for minor fixes
issue_body: This is just a suggestion regarding versioning. Given `{major}.{minor}.{patch}`, it may be worth considering using the `minor` version for releases, and thus freeing the `patch` version for hot fixes. For instance, having a release `0.1.0` allows for multiple very minor patches/fixes: `0.1.1`, `0.1.2`, `0.1.3`, etc.

issue_title: Question: When there are no valid taxons, we accept all taxons
issue_body: In `_validate_taxon()` if in the config there are no valid taxons, or `self.config.valid_taxa is None` we accept all taxons. This seems counterintuitive, since wouldn't that mean that there does not exist a taxon that is valid?

issue_title: Enabling widgets on jupyterhub for all users
issue_body:
Hi,

I am running jupyterhub with default local spawner installed via conda (system-wide installation). Jupyterhub is installed in its own coda-environment which also includes the single-user notebook. I provide another system environment to all users called `py36-sys`. I installed the ipywidgets package from conda-forge in the `py36-sys` environment. Still, users get an error when trying to use the widgets. 


I also tried to run this command after activating the `py36-sys` environment:

```
jupyter nbextension enable --py widgetsnbextension --sys-prefix
```

but the error persists.


**Test code:**
```python
from ipywidgets import interact

@interact(x=(0, 100, 10))
def p(x=50):
    pass
```
**Output**
```
Failed to display Jupyter Widget of type `interactive`.

If you're reading this message in the Jupyter Notebook or JupyterLab Notebook, it may mean that the widgets JavaScript is still loading. If this message persists, it likely means that the widgets JavaScript library is either not installed or not enabled. See the Jupyter Widgets Documentation for setup instructions.

If you're reading this message in another frontend (for example, a static rendering on GitHub or NBViewer), it may mean that your frontend doesn't currently support widgets.
```

issue_title: How to save widget state programmatically ?
issue_body:
In the current situation, as far I as know, widget state is not saved in the notebook by default.  
You must manually select the menu 'Widgets/Save widget state'.  

**Is there a way to save widget state from nbconvert ?**

(assuming nbconvert can deal with ipywidget 7.x- which can be done with manually with such a [template](https://github.com/oscar6echo/notebook-snapshot/blob/master/notebook_snapshot/templates/main.tpl.html) - waiting for nbconvert to catch up)

If not widgetized notebooks cannot be part of a batch work in which nbconvert typically:
+ copy a notebook
+ execute it
+ convert it to html (burning in images and removing code cells)

See this [notebook-snapshot](https://github.com/oscar6echo/notebook-snapshot) convenience package and its [demo nb](https://github.com/oscar6echo/notebook-snapshot/blob/master/demo_notebook_snapshot.ipynb) for an example of such unit workflow.

Is that already possible ? If not any workaround ?  
If not I think such a feature would add many use cases for widgets.

issue_title: display_data and update_display_data in widget output not working as expected
issue_body:
I am trying to update the output after a slider callback (see attached GIF). 
Other displays change as expected, but the one below the slider does not update.

![peek 2017-11-30 17-46](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/885054/33443048-e2ed606e-d5f6-11e7-8242-d6bbf7635c44.gif)

```python
# In[1]:
ip = get_ipython()
import ipywidgets as widgets
def display_with_id(obj, display_id=None, update=False):
    iopub = ip.kernel.iopub_socket
    session = ip.kernel.session
    data, md = ip.display_formatter.format(obj)
    transient = {'display_id': display_id}
    content = {'data': data, 'metadata': md, 'transient': transient}
    if display_id is None: content.pop('transient')  # make display_id option
    msg_type = 'update_display_data' if update else 'display_data'
    session.send(iopub, msg_type, content, parent=ip.parent_header)
display = display_with_id
# In[2]:
display('initial display', 'some_destination')
# In[11]:
display('spoiler alert: output updated in both', 'some_destination')
# In[4]:
display('no output here, update above', 'some_destination', update=True)
# In[5]:
w = widgets.IntSlider()
# In[6]:
display(w)
# In[7]:
w.value = 13
# In[8]:
first_disp = True
# In[9]:
def value_change(change):
    global first_disp
    if first_disp:
        display('ABCDEFG', 'some_destination', update=False)
        first_disp = False
    else:
        display("Not first anymore!", "some_destination", update=True)
# In[10]:
w.observe(value_change, names='value')
```

- [ ] Feature request (request for a new functionality)
- [x] Question
- [ ] Other

---



---

File: bot_filter.py
Line: 163
Code:

```python
def check_user(self, user_id, filter_closed_acc=False, unfollowing=False):
    if not self.filter_users and not unfollowing:
        console_print(self.verbosity,
                      '\n\033[91m filter_users is False , Skipping \033[0m')
        return True

    delay.small_delay(self)
    user_id = self.convert_to_user_id(user_id)

    if not user_id:
        console_print(self.verbosity,
                      '\n\033[91m not user_id , Skipping \033[0m')
        return False
    if self.whitelist and user_id in self.whitelist:
        console_print(self.verbosity,
                      '\n\033[92m user_id in self.whitelist \033[0m')
        return True
    if self.blacklist and user_id in self.blacklist:
        console_print(self.verbosity,
                      '\n\033[91m user_id in self.blacklist \033[0m')
        return False

    if not self.following:
        console_print(
            self.verbosity, '\n\033[92m Own following list is empty , downloading ...\033[0m')
        self.following = self.get_user_following(self.user_id)
    if user_id in self.following:
        # Log to Console
        console_print(self.verbosity,
                      '\n\033[91m Already following , Skipping \033[0m')
        return False

    user_info = self.get_user_info(user_id)
    if not user_info:
        console_print(self.verbosity,
                      '\n\033[91m not user_info , Skipping \033[0m')
        return False

    console_print(self.verbosity, '\n USER_NAME: %s , FOLLOWER: %s , '
                  'FOLLOWING: %s ' % (user_info["username"], user_info["follower_count"],
                                      user_info["following_count"]))

    if filter_closed_acc and "is_private" in user_info:
        if user_info["is_private"]:
            console_print(self.verbosity,
                          '\n info : \033[91m is PRIVATE , !!! \033[0m')
            return False
    if "is_business" in user_info:
        if user_info["is_business"]:
            console_print(self.verbosity,
                          '\n info : \033[91m is BUSINESS , Skipping \033[0m')
            skippedlist_adder(self, user_id)  # Add user_id to skipped.txt
            return False
```

What line codes or files do I need to change for the bot follow businesses?
The bot is skipping businesses accounts by default and it don't show me an option to change this filter.

issue_title: Enable variant effect prediction for models with multidimensional output
issue_body:
At the moment the scoring function can be user-defined, but the input to the scoring function has to be 1D already in the current setup. Requiring 1D is only necessary for the VCF file annotation really, so it would probably make sense to drop the 1D requirement for the general case and only require it when a VCF file should be annotated - in that case a nD -> 1D transformation could then either be applied to the model outputs (pre scoring function) or post scoring function...
We can define a set of "useful" nD -> 1D output conversions that can be used with these kinds of models.

issue_title: Reference exports (Model zoo)
issue_body:
It may be a good idea to add a set of models serialized in the `onnx.proto` format to the `onnx/examples` directory. This could serve as a reference for people testing ONNX or writing their own import/export functions. What do you think?

I would propose starting with a set of very simple models, getting progressively more and more complex: a simple addition (a+b+c), linear regression, logistic regression, MLP and then more advanced models such as AlexNet, VGG, ResNet, etc.

issue_title: Cleaner EBNF grammar
issue_body:
Would it be possible to modify the current ebnf grammar so instead the current
syntax where you're forced to have the first rule and colon after the rule name on
the same line:

    grammar = '''
        rule : rule1
            | rule2
    '''

you could have indented and clean blocks like this (fornmat used on glsl specs):

    grammar = '''
        rule : 
            rule1
            rule2
    '''

or maybe (not very clean one):

    grammar = '''
        rule : 
             | rule1
             | rule2
    '''

or:

    grammar = '''
        rule 
             : rule1
             | rule2
    '''

or (inspired from [Antlr4](https://github.com/antlr/grammars-v4) ):

    grammar = '''
        rule 
             : rule1
             | rule2
             ;
    '''

Rationale: That way the EBNF grammar will become much more readable and not only that, you'll be able to fold long grammars easily on your favourite text editor because the grammar now has proper indentation, example [here](https://dl.dropboxusercontent.com/s/jzvy9x1frpfmvz1/2018-05-26_04-15-24.mp4).

Guess it's a matter to tweak a bit this [file](https://github.com/lark-parser/lark/blob/master/lark/load_grammar.py), even if you don't like the idea, could you explain how you'd do so?

Thanks.

issue_title: api.content.move should not use keyword arguments
issue_body: I believe that `move` should not enforce the use of keyword arguments (which I think is the intention).  For the purpose of clarity, `move` and `rename` should be two different functions.  They may be similar in Unix, but the way that you have to call `move` with different keyword arguments to do either move or rename in Plone is utterly confusing to users, and I'd argue there is no advantage over using two functions.
